search_text: Feeling Optimistic? Ambiguity Attitudes for Online Decision Making 感觉乐观吗？在线决策的模糊态度 Due to the complexity of many decision making problems, tree search algorithms often have inadequate information to produce accurate transition models. This results in ambiguities (uncertainties for which there are multiple plausible models). Faced with ambiguities, robust methods have been used to produce safe solutions--often by maximizing the lower bound over the set of plausible transition models. However, they often overlook how much the representation of uncertainty can impact how a decision is made. This work introduces the Ambiguity Attitude Graph Search (AAGS), advocating for more comprehensive representations of ambiguities in decision making. Additionally, AAGS allows users to adjust their ambiguity attitude (or preference), promoting exploration and improving users' ability to control how an agent should respond when faced with a set of plausible alternatives. Simulation in a dynamic sailing environment shows how environments with high entropy transition models can lead robust methods to fail. Results further demonstrate how adjusting ambiguity attitudes better fulfills objectives while mitigating this failure mode of robust approaches. Because this approach is a generalization of the robust framework, these results further demonstrate how algorithms focused on ambiguity have applicability beyond safety-critical systems. 由于许多决策问题的复杂性，树搜索算法通常没有足够的信息来生成准确的转换模型。这会导致歧义（存在多个看似合理的模型的不确定性）。面对模糊性，稳健的方法已被用来产生安全的解决方案——通常是通过最大化一组合理的转换模型的下界。然而，他们常常忽视不确定性的表现对决策的影响有多大。这项工作引入了歧义态度图搜索（AAGS），提倡更全面地表示决策中的歧义。此外，AAGS 允许用户调整他们的模糊态度（或偏好），促进探索并提高用户控制代理在面对一组看似合理的替代方案时应如何响应的能力。动态航行环境中的模拟表明，具有高熵转换模型的环境如何导致鲁棒方法失败。结果进一步证明了调整模糊态度如何更好地实现目标，同时减轻稳健方法的这种失败模式。由于这种方法是鲁棒框架的概括，因此这些结果进一步证明了专注于模糊性的算法如何在安全关键系统之外具有适用性。 本文提出了一种名为Ambiguity Attitude Graph Search (AAGS)的新方法，用于在在线决策中更全面地表示和处理不确定性，允许用户调整对模糊性的态度以优化决策。 Jared J. Beard,R. Michael Butts,Yu Gu cs.RO

authors_text: Tianyuan Cai,Aleena Gertrudes Niklaus,Michael Kraley,Bernard Kerr,Zoya Bylinskii
title: THERIF: A Pipeline for Generating Themes for Readability with Iterative Feedback
title_zh: THERIF：通过迭代反馈生成可读性主题的管道
comment: Extended version of CHI LBW'2023 paper
comments: Extended version of CHI LBW'2023 paper
abstract: Digital reading applications give readers the ability to customize fonts, sizes, and spacings, all of which have been shown to improve the reading experience for readers from different demographics. However, tweaking these text features can be challenging, especially given their interactions on the final look and feel of the text. Our solution is to offer readers preset combinations of font, character, word and line spacing, which we bundle together into reading themes. To arrive at a recommended set of reading themes, we present our THERIF pipeline, which combines crowdsourced text adjustments, ML-driven clustering of text formats, and design sessions. We show that after four iterations of our pipeline, we converge on a set of three COR themes (Compact, Open, and Relaxed) that meet diverse readers' preferences, when evaluating the reading speeds, comprehension scores, and preferences of hundreds of readers with and without dyslexia, using crowdsourced experiments.
summary: Digital reading applications give readers the ability to customize fonts, sizes, and spacings, all of which have been shown to improve the reading experience for readers from different demographics. However, tweaking these text features can be challenging, especially given their interactions on the final look and feel of the text. Our solution is to offer readers preset combinations of font, character, word and line spacing, which we bundle together into reading themes. To arrive at a recommended set of reading themes, we present our THERIF pipeline, which combines crowdsourced text adjustments, ML-driven clustering of text formats, and design sessions. We show that after four iterations of our pipeline, we converge on a set of three COR themes (Compact, Open, and Relaxed) that meet diverse readers' preferences, when evaluating the reading speeds, comprehension scores, and preferences of hundreds of readers with and without dyslexia, using crowdsourced experiments.
abstract_zh: 数字阅读应用程序使读者能够自定义字体、大小和间距，所有这些都已被证明可以改善不同人群读者的阅读体验。然而，调整这些文本功能可能具有挑战性，特别是考虑到它们对文本的最终外观和感觉的相互作用。我们的解决方案是为读者提供字体、字符、单词和行距的预设组合，我们将它们捆绑在一起形成阅读主题。为了得出一组推荐的阅读主题，我们提出了 THERIF 管道，该管道结合了众包文本调整、机器学习驱动的文本格式聚类和设计会议。我们表明，在我们的流程经过四次迭代之后，在使用众包实验评估数百名有或没有阅读障碍的读者的阅读速度、理解分数和偏好时，我们收敛于一组满足不同读者偏好的三个 COR 主题（紧凑、开放和宽松）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出THERIF流水线，通过迭代反馈生成提升可读性的阅读主题，结合用户偏好与无障碍需求。
AI.motivation: 数字阅读应用中字体、间距等参数的自定义虽能提升阅读体验，但参数交互复杂，需预设主题满足多样化需求，尤其关注阅读障碍者。
AI.method: 融合众包文本调整、机器学习聚类及设计研讨会的THERIF流水线，经四轮迭代生成主题组合。
AI.result: 收敛出三类主题（紧凑型、开放型、放松型），通过数百名读者（含阅读障碍者）的阅读速度、理解度及偏好验证有效性。
AI.conclusion: THERIF流水线通过多轮迭代反馈，成功生成兼顾可读性与用户偏好的阅读主题，提升无障碍阅读体验。

title: Deep Occupancy-Predictive Representations for Autonomous Driving
title_zh: 自动驾驶的深度占用预测表示
abstract: Manually specifying features that capture the diversity in traffic environments is impractical. Consequently, learning-based agents cannot realize their full potential as neural motion planners for autonomous vehicles. Instead, this work proposes to learn which features are task-relevant. Given its immediate relevance to motion planning, our proposed architecture encodes the probabilistic occupancy map as a proxy for obtaining pre-trained state representations. By leveraging a map-aware graph formulation of the environment, our agent-centric encoder generalizes to arbitrary road networks and traffic situations. We show that our approach significantly improves the downstream performance of a reinforcement learning agent operating in urban traffic environments.
summary: Manually specifying features that capture the diversity in traffic environments is impractical. Consequently, learning-based agents cannot realize their full potential as neural motion planners for autonomous vehicles. Instead, this work proposes to learn which features are task-relevant. Given its immediate relevance to motion planning, our proposed architecture encodes the probabilistic occupancy map as a proxy for obtaining pre-trained state representations. By leveraging a map-aware graph formulation of the environment, our agent-centric encoder generalizes to arbitrary road networks and traffic situations. We show that our approach significantly improves the downstream performance of a reinforcement learning agent operating in urban traffic environments.
abstract_zh: 手动指定捕获流量环境多样性的特征是不切实际的。因此，基于学习的智能体无法充分发挥其作为自动驾驶车辆神经运动规划器的潜力。相反，这项工作建议了解哪些特征与任务相关。鉴于其与运动规划的直接相关性，我们提出的架构将概率占用图编码为获取预训练状态表示的代理。通过利用环境的地图感知图形公式，我们以代理为中心的编码器可推广到任意道路网络和交通状况。我们表明，我们的方法显着提高了在城市交通环境中运行的强化学习代理的下游性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度占用预测表示的方法，通过学习任务相关特征提升自动驾驶中运动规划的性能。
AI.motivation: 手动设计的特征难以捕捉交通环境的多样性，导致基于学习的智能体无法充分发挥作为自动驾驶神经运动规划器的潜力。
AI.method: 以概率占用图为代理，利用地图感知的图环境表示，设计代理中心的编码器以泛化到任意道路网络和交通场景。
AI.result: 该方法显著提升了强化学习代理在城市交通环境中的下游任务性能。
AI.conclusion: 通过自动学习任务相关特征，本文方法有效增强了自动驾驶系统的运动规划能力。

motivation: 手动设计的特征难以捕捉交通环境的多样性，导致基于学习的智能体无法充分发挥作为自动驾驶神经运动规划器的潜力。
method: 以概率占用图为代理，利用地图感知的图环境表示，设计代理中心的编码器以泛化到任意道路网络和交通场景。
result: 该方法显著提升了强化学习代理在城市交通环境中的下游任务性能。
conclusion: 通过自动学习任务相关特征，本文方法有效增强了自动驾驶系统的运动规划能力。
search_text: Deep Occupancy-Predictive Representations for Autonomous Driving 自动驾驶的深度占用预测表示 Manually specifying features that capture the diversity in traffic environments is impractical. Consequently, learning-based agents cannot realize their full potential as neural motion planners for autonomous vehicles. Instead, this work proposes to learn which features are task-relevant. Given its immediate relevance to motion planning, our proposed architecture encodes the probabilistic occupancy map as a proxy for obtaining pre-trained state representations. By leveraging a map-aware graph formulation of the environment, our agent-centric encoder generalizes to arbitrary road networks and traffic situations. We show that our approach significantly improves the downstream performance of a reinforcement learning agent operating in urban traffic environments. 手动指定捕获流量环境多样性的特征是不切实际的。因此，基于学习的智能体无法充分发挥其作为自动驾驶车辆神经运动规划器的潜力。相反，这项工作建议了解哪些特征与任务相关。鉴于其与运动规划的直接相关性，我们提出的架构将概率占用图编码为获取预训练状态表示的代理。通过利用环境的地图感知图形公式，我们以代理为中心的编码器可推广到任意道路网络和交通状况。我们表明，我们的方法显着提高了在城市交通环境中运行的强化学习代理的下游性能。 本文提出一种基于深度占用预测表示的方法，通过学习任务相关特征提升自动驾驶中运动规划的性能。 Eivind Meyer,Lars Frederik Peiss,Matthias Althoff cs.LG,cs.RO

authors_text: Philipp Berens,Kyle Cranmer,Neil D. Lawrence,Ulrike von Luxburg,Jessica Montgomery
title: AI for Science: An Emerging Agenda
title_zh: 人工智能促进科学：新兴议程
abstract: This report documents the programme and the outcomes of Dagstuhl Seminar 22382 "Machine Learning for Science: Bridging Data-Driven and Mechanistic Modelling". Today's scientific challenges are characterised by complexity. Interconnected natural, technological, and human systems are influenced by forces acting across time- and spatial-scales, resulting in complex interactions and emergent behaviours. Understanding these phenomena -- and leveraging scientific advances to deliver innovative solutions to improve society's health, wealth, and well-being -- requires new ways of analysing complex systems. The transformative potential of AI stems from its widespread applicability across disciplines, and will only be achieved through integration across research domains. AI for science is a rendezvous point. It brings together expertise from $\mathrm{AI}$ and application domains; combines modelling knowledge with engineering know-how; and relies on collaboration across disciplines and between humans and machines. Alongside technical advances, the next wave of progress in the field will come from building a community of machine learning researchers, domain experts, citizen scientists, and engineers working together to design and deploy effective AI tools. This report summarises the discussions from the seminar and provides a roadmap to suggest how different communities can collaborate to deliver a new wave of progress in AI and its application for scientific discovery.

abstract_zh: 本报告记录了 Dagstuhl 研讨会 22382“科学机器学习：桥接数据驱动和机械建模”的计划和成果。当今的科学挑战具有复杂性的特点。相互关联的自然、技术和人类系统受到跨时间和空间尺度作用的力量的影响，导致复杂的相互作用和突发行为。了解这些现象并利用科学进步提供创新解决方案来改善社会的健康、财富和福祉，需要采用新的方法来分析复杂系统。人工智能的变革潜力源于其跨学科的广泛适用性，并且只有通过跨研究领域的整合才能实现。科学人工智能是一个交汇点。它汇集了来自 $\mathrm{AI}$ 和应用领域的专业知识；将建模知识与工程知识相结合；并依赖于跨学科以及人与机器之间的协作。除了技术进步之外，该领域的下一波进步将来自于建立一个由机器学习研究人员、领域专家、公民科学家和工程师组成的社区，共同设计和部署有效的人工智能工具。本报告总结了研讨会的讨论，并提供了一个路线图，建议不同社区如何合作，推动人工智能及其科学发现应用的新一波进展。

AI.tldr: 本文总结了Dagstuhl研讨会关于机器学习在科学领域应用的讨论，提出了跨学科协作推动AI科学发展的路线图。
AI.motivation: 现代科学挑战日益复杂，需通过AI整合数据驱动与机制建模方法，跨领域协作以解决复杂系统问题。
AI.method: 通过研讨会形式汇聚AI专家、领域学者及工程师，讨论技术进展与合作模式，形成发展路线图。
AI.result: 提出AI与科学融合的协作框架，明确技术突破与社区共建方向，为后续科研提供指导。
AI.conclusion: AI科学进步依赖跨学科协作，需建立包含研究人员、领域专家与公众的共同体以推动创新。

authors_text: Yue Meng,Sai Vemprala,Rogerio Bonatti,Chuchu Fan,Ashish Kapoor
title: ConBaT: Control Barrier Transformer for Safe Policy Learning
title_zh: ComBaT：用于安全策略学习的控制屏障变压器
abstract: Large-scale self-supervised models have recently revolutionized our ability to perform a variety of tasks within the vision and language domains. However, using such models for autonomous systems is challenging because of safety requirements: besides executing correct actions, an autonomous agent must also avoid the high cost and potentially fatal critical mistakes. Traditionally, self-supervised training mainly focuses on imitating previously observed behaviors, and the training demonstrations carry no notion of which behaviors should be explicitly avoided. In this work, we propose Control Barrier Transformer (ConBaT), an approach that learns safe behaviors from demonstrations in a self-supervised fashion. ConBaT is inspired by the concept of control barrier functions in control theory and uses a causal transformer that learns to predict safe robot actions autoregressively using a critic that requires minimal safety data labeling. During deployment, we employ a lightweight online optimization to find actions that ensure future states lie within the learned safe set. We apply our approach to different simulated control tasks and show that our method results in safer control policies compared to other classical and learning-based methods such as imitation learning, reinforcement learning, and model predictive control.
summary: Large-scale self-supervised models have recently revolutionized our ability to perform a variety of tasks within the vision and language domains. However, using such models for autonomous systems is challenging because of safety requirements: besides executing correct actions, an autonomous agent must also avoid the high cost and potentially fatal critical mistakes. Traditionally, self-supervised training mainly focuses on imitating previously observed behaviors, and the training demonstrations carry no notion of which behaviors should be explicitly avoided. In this work, we propose Control Barrier Transformer (ConBaT), an approach that learns safe behaviors from demonstrations in a self-supervised fashion. ConBaT is inspired by the concept of control barrier functions in control theory and uses a causal transformer that learns to predict safe robot actions autoregressively using a critic that requires minimal safety data labeling. During deployment, we employ a lightweight online optimization to find actions that ensure future states lie within the learned safe set. We apply our approach to different simulated control tasks and show that our method results in safer control policies compared to other classical and learning-based methods such as imitation learning, reinforcement learning, and model predictive control.
abstract_zh: 大规模自监督模型最近彻底改变了我们在视觉和语言领域执行各种任务的能力。然而，由于安全要求，将此类模型用于自主系统具有挑战性：除了执行正确的操作之外，自主代理还必须避免高成本和潜在致命的严重错误。传统上，自监督训练主要侧重于模仿先前观察到的行为，并且训练演示没有明确应该避免哪些行为的概念。在这项工作中，我们提出了控制屏障变压器（ConBaT），这是一种以自我监督的方式从演示中学习安全行为的方法。 ConBaT 受到控制理论中控制屏障函数概念的启发，并使用因果变换器，该变换器使用需要最少安全数据标记的批评器来学习自回归预测安全机器人动作。在部署过程中，我们采用轻量级在线优化来寻找确保未来状态位于学习的安全集中的操作。我们将我们的方法应用于不同的模拟控制任务，并表明与其他经典和基于学习的方法（例如模仿学习、强化学习和模型预测控制）相比，我们的方法可以产生更安全的控制策略。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ConBaT方法，通过自监督学习从演示中获取安全行为策略，解决自主系统安全控制问题。
AI.motivation: 传统自监督模型无法满足自主系统对安全性的要求，现有方法缺乏对危险行为的显式规避机制。
AI.method: 结合控制屏障函数理论与因果变压器架构，通过自回归预测安全动作并采用轻量级在线优化确保状态安全。
AI.result: 在多个模拟控制任务中，ConBaT相较模仿学习、强化学习等方法展现出更优的安全性表现。
AI.conclusion: ConBaT为自主系统提供了有效的安全策略学习框架，可广泛应用于机器人控制等安全敏感场景。

authors_text: Joshua R. Loftus,Lucius E. J. Bynum,Sakina Hansen
title: Causal Dependence Plots
title_zh: 因果相关图
abstract: Explaining artificial intelligence or machine learning models is increasingly important. To use such data-driven systems wisely we must understand how they interact with the world, including how they depend causally on data inputs. In this work we develop Causal Dependence Plots (CDPs) to visualize how one variable--an outcome--depends on changes in another variable--a predictor--$\textit{along with any consequent causal changes in other predictor variables}$. Crucially, CDPs differ from standard methods based on holding other predictors constant or assuming they are independent. CDPs make use of an auxiliary causal model because causal conclusions require causal assumptions. With simulations and real data experiments, we show CDPs can be combined in a modular way with methods for causal learning or sensitivity analysis. Since people often think causally about input-output dependence, CDPs can be powerful tools in the xAI or interpretable machine learning toolkit and contribute to applications like scientific machine learning and algorithmic fairness.
summary: Explaining artificial intelligence or machine learning models is increasingly important. To use such data-driven systems wisely we must understand how they interact with the world, including how they depend causally on data inputs. In this work we develop Causal Dependence Plots (CDPs) to visualize how one variable--an outcome--depends on changes in another variable--a predictor--$\textit{along with any consequent causal changes in other predictor variables}$. Crucially, CDPs differ from standard methods based on holding other predictors constant or assuming they are independent. CDPs make use of an auxiliary causal model because causal conclusions require causal assumptions. With simulations and real data experiments, we show CDPs can be combined in a modular way with methods for causal learning or sensitivity analysis. Since people often think causally about input-output dependence, CDPs can be powerful tools in the xAI or interpretable machine learning toolkit and contribute to applications like scientific machine learning and algorithmic fairness.
abstract_zh: 解释人工智能或机器学习模型变得越来越重要。要明智地使用此类数据驱动系统，我们必须了解它们如何与世界交互，包括它们如何因果地依赖于数据输入。在这项工作中，我们开发了因果相关图（CDP）来可视化一个变量（结果）如何依赖于另一个变量（预测变量）$\textit{以及其他预测变量中随之而来的因果变化}$的变化。至关重要的是，CDP 与基于保持其他预测变量不变或假设它们是独立的标准方法不同。 CDP 使用辅助因果模型，因为因果结论需要因果假设。通过模拟和真实数据实验，我们表明 CDP 可以以模块化方式与因果学习或敏感性分析方法相结合。由于人们经常以因果关系来思考输入输出依赖性，因此 CDP 可以成为 xAI 或可解释机器学习工具包中的强大工具，并有助于科学机器学习和算法公平性等应用。

AI.tldr: 开发因果依赖图（CDPs）以可视化结果变量对预测变量的因果依赖关系，并考虑其他预测变量的因果变化。
AI.motivation: 为更好地理解和解释AI模型如何与现实世界互动，特别是其因果依赖关系，传统方法存在局限性，需要新的可视化工具。
AI.method: 通过构建辅助因果模型，结合因果学习和敏感性分析方法，生成CDPs以展示变量间的因果依赖关系。
AI.result: 实验表明CDPs能有效结合其他方法，适用于科学机器学习和算法公平性等领域，提升模型解释性。
AI.conclusion: CDPs为因果解释提供了有效工具，有助于提升AI模型的透明度和公平性，推动科学机器学习发展。

search_text: Causal Dependence Plots 因果相关图 Explaining artificial intelligence or machine learning models is increasingly important. To use such data-driven systems wisely we must understand how they interact with the world, including how they depend causally on data inputs. In this work we develop Causal Dependence Plots (CDPs) to visualize how one variable--an outcome--depends on changes in another variable--a predictor--$\textit{along with any consequent causal changes in other predictor variables}$. Crucially, CDPs differ from standard methods based on holding other predictors constant or assuming they are independent. CDPs make use of an auxiliary causal model because causal conclusions require causal assumptions. With simulations and real data experiments, we show CDPs can be combined in a modular way with methods for causal learning or sensitivity analysis. Since people often think causally about input-output dependence, CDPs can be powerful tools in the xAI or interpretable machine learning toolkit and contribute to applications like scientific machine learning and algorithmic fairness. 解释人工智能或机器学习模型变得越来越重要。要明智地使用此类数据驱动系统，我们必须了解它们如何与世界交互，包括它们如何因果地依赖于数据输入。在这项工作中，我们开发了因果相关图（CDP）来可视化一个变量（结果）如何依赖于另一个变量（预测变量）$\textit{以及其他预测变量中随之而来的因果变化}$的变化。至关重要的是，CDP 与基于保持其他预测变量不变或假设它们是独立的标准方法不同。 CDP 使用辅助因果模型，因为因果结论需要因果假设。通过模拟和真实数据实验，我们表明 CDP 可以以模块化方式与因果学习或敏感性分析方法相结合。由于人们经常以因果关系来思考输入输出依赖性，因此 CDP 可以成为 xAI 或可解释机器学习工具包中的强大工具，并有助于科学机器学习和算法公平性等应用。 开发因果依赖图（CDPs）以可视化结果变量对预测变量的因果依赖关系，并考虑其他预测变量的因果变化。 Joshua R. Loftus,Lucius E. J. Bynum,Sakina Hansen cs.LG,cs.AI,stat.ML

title: EscherNet 101
title_zh: 埃舍尔网101
abstract: A deep learning model, EscherNet 101, is constructed to categorize images of 2D periodic patterns into their respective 17 wallpaper groups. Beyond evaluating EscherNet 101 performance by classification rates, at a micro-level we investigate the filters learned at different layers in the network, capable of capturing second-order invariants beyond edge and curvature.
summary: A deep learning model, EscherNet 101, is constructed to categorize images of 2D periodic patterns into their respective 17 wallpaper groups. Beyond evaluating EscherNet 101 performance by classification rates, at a micro-level we investigate the filters learned at different layers in the network, capable of capturing second-order invariants beyond edge and curvature.
abstract_zh: 构建深度学习模型 EscherNet 101，将 2D 周期性图案的图像分类为各自的 17 个壁纸组。除了通过分类率评估 EscherNet 101 性能之外，我们还在微观层面上研究了在网络不同层学习的滤波器，这些滤波器能够捕获边缘和曲率之外的二阶不变量。

AI.tldr: 本文构建了深度学习模型EscherNet 101，用于将二维周期性图案图像分类到17种墙纸群，并分析网络滤波器对二阶不变量的捕捉能力。
AI.motivation: 传统方法难以有效识别二维周期图案的高阶几何特征，需通过深度学习模型挖掘更复杂的不变量以提升分类性能。
AI.method: 设计深度神经网络EscherNet 101，通过多层卷积滤波器学习图像特征，并分析不同网络层对二阶不变量的提取能力。
AI.result: 模型在分类任务中表现出较高准确率，且微观分析显示滤波器能有效捕捉超越边缘和曲率的二阶几何不变量。
AI.conclusion: EscherNet 101验证了深度学习在二维周期图案分类中的有效性，揭示了网络滤波器对高阶几何特征的建模能力。

authors_text: Marilia D. V. Braga,Leonie R. Brockmann,Katharina Klerx,Jens Stoye
title: Investigating the complexity of the double distance problems
title_zh: 研究双距离问题的复杂性
abstract: Two genomes over the same set of gene families form a canonical pair when each of them has exactly one gene from each family. Different distances of canonical genomes can be derived from a structure called breakpoint graph, which represents the relation between the two given genomes as a collection of cycles of even length and paths. Then, the breakpoint distance is equal to n - (c_2 + p_0/2), where n is the number of genes, c_2 is the number of cycles of length 2 and p_0 is the number of paths of length 0. Similarly, when the considered rearrangements are those modeled by the double-cut-and-join (DCJ) operation, the rearrangement distance is n - (c + p_e/2), where c is the total number of cycles and p_e is the total number of even paths. The distance formulation is a basic unit for several other combinatorial problems related to genome evolution and ancestral reconstruction, such as median or double distance. Interestingly, both median and double distance problems can be solved in polynomial time for the breakpoint distance, while they are NP-hard for the rearrangement distance. One way of exploring the complexity space between these two extremes is to consider the σ_k distance, defined to be n - [c_2 + c_4 + ... + c_k + (p_0 + p_2 + ... +p_k)/2], and increasingly investigate the complexities of median and double distance for the σ_4 distance, then the σ_6 distance, and so on. While for the median much effort was done in our and in other research groups but no progress was obtained even for the σ_4 distance, for solving the double distance under σ_4 and σ_6 distances we could devise linear time algorithms, which we present here.
summary: Two genomes over the same set of gene families form a canonical pair when each of them has exactly one gene from each family. Different distances of canonical genomes can be derived from a structure called breakpoint graph, which represents the relation between the two given genomes as a collection of cycles of even length and paths. Then, the breakpoint distance is equal to n - (c_2 + p_0/2), where n is the number of genes, c_2 is the number of cycles of length 2 and p_0 is the number of paths of length 0. Similarly, when the considered rearrangements are those modeled by the double-cut-and-join (DCJ) operation, the rearrangement distance is n - (c + p_e/2), where c is the total number of cycles and p_e is the total number of even paths. The distance formulation is a basic unit for several other combinatorial problems related to genome evolution and ancestral reconstruction, such as median or double distance. Interestingly, both median and double distance problems can be solved in polynomial time for the breakpoint distance, while they are NP-hard for the rearrangement distance. One way of exploring the complexity space between these two extremes is to consider the σ_k distance, defined to be n - [c_2 + c_4 + ... + c_k + (p_0 + p_2 + ... +p_k)/2], and increasingly investigate the complexities of median and double distance for the σ_4 distance, then the σ_6 distance, and so on. While for the median much effort was done in our and in other research groups but no progress was obtained even for the σ_4 distance, for solving the double distance under σ_4 and σ_6 distances we could devise linear time algorithms, which we present here.
abstract_zh: 当同一组基因家族中的两个基因组中的每个基因组都恰好具有来自每个家族的一个基因时，它们就形成了规范对。规范基因组的不同距离可以从一种称为断点图的结构中得出，该结构将两个给定基因组之间的关系表示为偶数长度和路径的循环的集合。那么，断点距离等于 n - (c_2 + p_0/2)，其中 n 是基因数量，c_2 是长度为 2 的循环数，p_0 是长度为 0 的路径数。类似地，当考虑的重排是通过双切连接 (DCJ) 操作建模的重排时，重排距离为 n - (c + p_e/2)，其中 c 是循环总数，p_e 是总循环数偶数路径的数量。距离公式是与基因组进化和祖先重建相关的其他几个组合问题的基本单位，例如中值或双倍距离。有趣的是，对于断点距离，中值距离问题和双倍距离问题都可以在多项式时间内解决，而对于重排距离来说，它们是 NP 困难的。探索这两个极端之间的复杂性空间的一种方法是考虑 σ_k 距离，定义为 n - [c_2 + c_4 + ... + c_k + (p_0 + p_2 + ... +p_k)/2]，并越来越多地研究 σ_4 距离的中值和双倍距离的复杂性，然后是 σ_6 距离，依此类推。虽然我们和其他研究小组为中位数做了很多努力，但即使对于 σ_4 距离也没有取得任何进展，为了解决 σ_4 和 σ_6 距离下的双倍距离，我们可以设计线性时间算法，我们在此介绍。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了基于σ_k距离的双距离问题的复杂性，提出了针对σ_4和σ_6距离的线性时间算法。
AI.motivation: 基因组演化中的距离计算存在多项式时间与NP难问题的复杂性差异，需探索中间距离模型的算法可行性。
AI.method: 通过分析断点图结构，结合σ_k距离定义，设计针对特定路径和循环结构的线性时间算法。
AI.result: 成功开发了σ_4和σ_6距离下的双距离问题线性时间算法，填补了σ_2与σ_∞间的复杂性研究空白。
AI.conclusion: 特定σ_k距离模型可实现高效双距离计算，为基因组祖先重建等应用提供新方法论支持。

ai_content.tldr: 本文研究了基于σ_k距离的双距离问题的复杂性，提出了针对σ_4和σ_6距离的线性时间算法。

ai_content.result: 成功开发了σ_4和σ_6距离下的双距离问题线性时间算法，填补了σ_2与σ_∞间的复杂性研究空白。
ai_content.conclusion: 特定σ_k距离模型可实现高效双距离计算，为基因组祖先重建等应用提供新方法论支持。

While for the median much effort was done in our and in other research groups but no progress was obtained even for the σ_4 distance, for solving the double distance under σ_4 and σ_6 distances we could devise linear time algorithms, which we present here. 当同一组基因家族中的两个基因组中的每个基因组都恰好具有来自每个家族的一个基因时，它们就形成了规范对。规范基因组的不同距离可以从一种称为断点图的结构中得出，该结构将两个给定基因组之间的关系表示为偶数长度和路径的循环的集合。那么，断点距离等于 n - (c_2 + p_0/2)，其中 n 是基因数量，c_2 是长度为 2 的循环数，p_0 是长度为 0 的路径数。类似地，当考虑的重排是通过双切连接 (DCJ) 操作建模的重排时，重排距离为 n - (c + p_e/2)，其中 c 是循环总数，p_e 是总循环数偶数路径的数量。距离公式是与基因组进化和祖先重建相关的其他几个组合问题的基本单位，例如中值或双倍距离。有趣的是，对于断点距离，中值距离问题和双倍距离问题都可以在多项式时间内解决，而对于重排距离来说，它们是 NP 困难的。探索这两个极端之间的复杂性空间的一种方法是考虑 σ_k 距离，定义为 n - [c_2 + c_4 + ... + c_k + (p_0 + p_2 + ... +p_k)/2]，并越来越多地研究 σ_4 距离的中值和双倍距离的复杂性，然后是 σ_6 距离，依此类推。虽然我们和其他研究小组为中位数做了很多努力，但即使对于 σ_4 距离也没有取得任何进展，为了解决 σ_4 和 σ_6 距离下的双倍距离，我们可以设计线性时间算法，我们在此介绍。 本文研究了基于σ_k距离的双距离问题的复杂性，提出了针对σ_4和σ_6距离的线性时间算法。 Marilia D. V. Braga,Leonie R. Brockmann,Katharina Klerx,Jens Stoye cs.DS

authors_text: Qingyi Wang,Shenhao Wang,Yunhan Zheng,Hongzhou Lin,Xiaohu Zhang,Jinhua Zhao,Joan Walker
title: Deep hybrid model with satellite imagery: how to combine demand modeling and computer vision for behavior analysis?
title_zh: 卫星图像深度混合模型：如何结合需求建模和计算机视觉进行行为分析？
comment: ef:Transportation Research Part B: Methodological, Volume 179, 2024, 102869
comments: ef:Transportation Research Part B: Methodological, Volume 179, 2024, 102869
abstract: Classical demand modeling analyzes travel behavior using only low-dimensional numeric data (i.e. sociodemographics and travel attributes) but not high-dimensional urban imagery. However, travel behavior depends on the factors represented by both numeric data and urban imagery, thus necessitating a synergetic framework to combine them. This study creates a theoretical framework of deep hybrid models with a crossing structure consisting of a mixing operator and a behavioral predictor, thus integrating the numeric and imagery data into a latent space. Empirically, this framework is applied to analyze travel mode choice using the MyDailyTravel Survey from Chicago as the numeric inputs and the satellite images as the imagery inputs. We found that deep hybrid models outperform both the traditional demand models and the recent deep learning in predicting the aggregate and disaggregate travel behavior with our supervision-as-mixing design. The latent space in deep hybrid models can be interpreted, because it reveals meaningful spatial and social patterns. The deep hybrid models can also generate new urban images that do not exist in reality and interpret them with economic theory, such as computing substitution patterns and social welfare changes. Overall, the deep hybrid models demonstrate the complementarity between the low-dimensional numeric and high-dimensional imagery data and between the traditional demand modeling and recent deep learning. It generalizes the latent classes and variables in classical hybrid demand models to a latent space, and leverages the computational power of deep learning for imagery while retaining the economic interpretability on the microeconomics foundation.

However, travel behavior depends on the factors represented by both numeric data and urban imagery, thus necessitating a synergetic framework to combine them. This study creates a theoretical framework of deep hybrid models with a crossing structure consisting of a mixing operator and a behavioral predictor, thus integrating the numeric and imagery data into a latent space. Empirically, this framework is applied to analyze travel mode choice using the MyDailyTravel Survey from Chicago as the numeric inputs and the satellite images as the imagery inputs. We found that deep hybrid models outperform both the traditional demand models and the recent deep learning in predicting the aggregate and disaggregate travel behavior with our supervision-as-mixing design. The latent space in deep hybrid models can be interpreted, because it reveals meaningful spatial and social patterns. The deep hybrid models can also generate new urban images that do not exist in reality and interpret them with economic theory, such as computing substitution patterns and social welfare changes. Overall, the deep hybrid models demonstrate the complementarity between the low-dimensional numeric and high-dimensional imagery data and between the traditional demand modeling and recent deep learning. It generalizes the latent classes and variables in classical hybrid demand models to a latent space, and leverages the computational power of deep learning for imagery while retaining the economic interpretability on the microeconomics foundation.
abstract_zh: 经典需求建模仅使用低维数字数据（即社会人口统计和旅行属性）而不是高维城市图像来分析旅行行为。然而，出行行为取决于数字数据和城市图像所代表的因素，因此需要一个协同框架将它们结合起来。这项研究创建了深度混合模型的理论框架，其交叉结构由混合算子和行为预测器组成，从而将数字和图像数据集成到潜在空间中。根据经验，该框架用于分析出行模式选择，使用芝加哥的 MyDailyTravel 调查作为数字输入，卫星图像作为图像输入。我们发现，深度混合模型在通过我们的监督混合设计来预测聚合和分类出行行为方面优于传统需求模型和最近的深度学习。深度混合模型中的潜在空间是可以解释的，因为它揭示了有意义的空间和社会模式。深度混合模型还可以生成现实中不存在的新城市图像，并用经济理论对其进行解释，例如计算替代模式和社会福利变化。总体而言，深度混合模型证明了低维数字数据和高维图像数据之间以及传统需求建模和最新深度学习之间的互补性。它将经典混合需求模型中的潜在类别和变量推广到潜在空间，并利用深度学习的计算能力进行图像处理，同时保留微观经济学基础上的经济可解释性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合数值数据与卫星图像的深度混合模型，用于分析出行行为。
AI.motivation: 传统需求模型仅依赖低维数值数据，无法捕捉高维城市图像信息，而出行行为受两者共同影响，需构建协同框架整合多源数据。
AI.method: 设计包含混合运算符和行为预测器的深度混合模型，将数值数据与卫星图像映射至潜在空间，并通过实证分析验证模型效果。
AI.result: 混合模型在预测聚合与离散出行行为上优于传统模型和深度学习方法，潜在空间可解释空间社会模式并生成符合经济理论的虚拟城市图像。
AI.conclusion: 深度混合模型证明了低维数值数据与高维图像数据的互补性，融合传统需求建模与深度学习优势，提升行为分析的准确性与可解释性。

title: What is Memory? A Homological Perspective
title_zh: 什么是记忆？同源观点
abstract: We introduce the delta-homology model of memory, a unified framework in which recall, learning, and prediction emerge from cycle closure, the completion of topologically constrained trajectories within the brain's latent manifold. A Dirac-like memory trace corresponds to a nontrivial homology generator, representing a sparse, irreducible attractor that reactivates only when inference trajectories close upon themselves. In this view, memory is not a static attractor landscape but a topological process of recurrence, where structure arises through the stabilization of closed loops. Building on this principle, we represent spike-timing dynamics as spatiotemporal complexes, in which temporally consistent transitions among neurons form chain complexes supporting persistent activation cycles. These cycles are organized into cell posets, compact causal representations that encode overlapping and compositional memory traces. Within this construction, learning and recall correspond to cycle closure under contextual modulation: inference trajectories stabilize into nontrivial homology classes when both local synchrony (context) and global recurrence (content) are satisfied. We formalize this mechanism through the Context-Content Uncertainty Principle (CCUP), which states that cognition minimizes joint uncertainty between a high-entropy context variable and a low-entropy content variable. Synchronization acts as a context filter selecting coherent subnetworks, while recurrence acts as a content filter validating nontrivial cycles.
summary: We introduce the delta-homology model of memory, a unified framework in which recall, learning, and prediction emerge from cycle closure, the completion of topologically constrained trajectories within the brain's latent manifold. A Dirac-like memory trace corresponds to a nontrivial homology generator, representing a sparse, irreducible attractor that reactivates only when inference trajectories close upon themselves. In this view, memory is not a static attractor landscape but a topological process of recurrence, where structure arises through the stabilization of closed loops. Building on this principle, we represent spike-timing dynamics as spatiotemporal complexes, in which temporally consistent transitions among neurons form chain complexes supporting persistent activation cycles. These cycles are organized into cell posets, compact causal representations that encode overlapping and compositional memory traces. Within this construction, learning and recall correspond to cycle closure under contextual modulation: inference trajectories stabilize into nontrivial homology classes when both local synchrony (context) and global recurrence (content) are satisfied. We formalize this mechanism through the Context-Content Uncertainty Principle (CCUP), which states that cognition minimizes joint uncertainty between a high-entropy context variable and a low-entropy content variable. Synchronization acts as a context filter selecting coherent subnetworks, while recurrence acts as a content filter validating nontrivial cycles.
abstract_zh: 我们引入了记忆的δ同源模型，这是一个统一的框架，其中回忆、学习和预测是从循环闭合中产生的，即大脑潜在流形内拓扑约束轨迹的完成。类似狄拉克的记忆轨迹对应于一个不平凡的同源生成器，代表一个稀疏的、不可约的吸引子，只有当推理轨迹接近时才会重新激活。按照这种观点，记忆不是一个静态的吸引子景观，而是一个循环的拓扑过程，其中结构是通过闭环的稳定而产生的。基于这一原理，我们将尖峰时序动力学表示为时空复合体，其中神经元之间时间一致的转变形成支持持续激活周期的链复合体。这些循环被组织成细胞偏序集，即编码重叠和组合记忆痕迹的紧凑因果表示。在这种结构中，学​​习和回忆对应于上下文调制下的循环闭合：当局部同步（上下文）和全局递归（内容）都得到满足时，推理轨迹稳定为非平凡的同源类。我们通过上下文内容不确定性原理（CCUP）形式化了这种机制，该原理指出认知最大限度地减少了高熵上下文变量和低熵内容变量之间的联合不确定性。同步充当选择相干子网络的上下文过滤器，而循环充当验证重要循环的内容过滤器。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于同调论的delta-同调记忆模型，将记忆视为大脑潜空间中拓扑约束轨迹的闭合循环过程，并通过上下文-内容不确定性原理（CCUP）解释学习与回忆的机制。
AI.motivation: 传统记忆模型难以统一解释回忆、学习与预测的动态过程，需引入拓扑学方法揭示记忆的结构化生成机制。
AI.method: 将神经元放电动态建模为时空复合体，利用同调论中的非平凡生成元描述稀疏吸引子，并通过上下文同步与全局循环的相互作用定义CCUP原理。
AI.result: 构建了记忆的拓扑过程框架，证明记忆痕迹通过闭合循环稳定化，且CCUP原理能形式化认知中上下文与内容的不确定性平衡。
AI.conclusion: 记忆本质是拓扑递归过程，其结构源于闭合环路的稳定化，该模型为理解认知机制提供了新的数学工具与理论视角。

Within this construction, learning and recall correspond to cycle closure under contextual modulation: inference trajectories stabilize into nontrivial homology classes when both local synchrony (context) and global recurrence (content) are satisfied. Synchronization acts as a context filter selecting coherent subnetworks, while recurrence acts as a content filter validating nontrivial cycles. 我们引入了记忆的δ同源模型，这是一个统一的框架，其中回忆、学习和预测是从循环闭合中产生的，即大脑潜在流形内拓扑约束轨迹的完成。类似狄拉克的记忆轨迹对应于一个不平凡的同源生成器，代表一个稀疏的、不可约的吸引子，只有当推理轨迹接近时才会重新激活。按照这种观点，记忆不是一个静态的吸引子景观，而是一个循环的拓扑过程，其中结构是通过闭环的稳定而产生的。基于这一原理，我们将尖峰时序动力学表示为时空复合体，其中神经元之间时间一致的转变形成支持持续激活周期的链复合体。这些循环被组织成细胞偏序集，即编码重叠和组合记忆痕迹的紧凑因果表示。在这种结构中，学​​习和回忆对应于上下文调制下的循环闭合：当局部同步（上下文）和全局递归（内容）都得到满足时，推理轨迹稳定为非平凡的同源类。我们通过上下文内容不确定性原理（CCUP）形式化了这种机制，该原理指出认知最大限度地减少了高熵上下文变量和低熵内容变量之间的联合不确定性。同步充当选择相干子网络的上下文过滤器，而循环充当验证重要循环的内容过滤器。 本文提出了一种基于同调论的delta-同调记忆模型，将记忆视为大脑潜空间中拓扑约束轨迹的闭合循环过程，并通过上下文-内容不确定性原理（CCUP）解释学习与回忆的机制。 Xin Li cs.LG,cs.CV

title: Diversity Embeddings and the Hypergraph Sparsest Cut
title_zh: 多样性嵌入和超图稀疏切割
abstract: Good approximations have been attained for the sparsest cut problem by rounding solutions to convex relaxations via low-distortion metric embeddings. Recently, Bryant and Tupper showed that this approach extends to the hypergraph setting by formulating a linear program whose solutions are so-called diversities which are rounded via diversity embeddings into $\ell_1$. Diversities are a generalization of metric spaces in which the nonnegative function is defined on all subsets as opposed to only on pairs of elements. We show that this approach yields a polytime $O(\log{n})$-approximation when either the supply or demands are given by a graph. This result improves upon Plotkin et al.'s $O(\log{(kn)}\log{n})$-approximation, where $k$ is the number of demands, for the setting where the supply is given by a graph and the demands are given by a hypergraph. Additionally, we provide a polytime $O(\min{\{r_G,r_H\}}\log{r_H}\log{n})$-approximation for when the supply and demands are given by hypergraphs whose hyperedges are bounded in cardinality by $r_G$ and $r_H$ respectively. To establish these results we provide an $O(\log{n})$-distortion $\ell_1$ embedding for the class of diversities known as diameter diversities. This improves upon Bryant and Tupper's $O(\log\^2{n})$-distortion embedding. The smallest known distortion with which an arbitrary diversity can be embedded into $\ell_1$ is $O(n)$. We show that for any $ε> 0$ and any $p>0$, there is a family of diversities which cannot be embedded into $\ell_1$ in polynomial time with distortion smaller than $O(n^{1-ε})$ based on querying the diversities on sets of cardinality at most $O(\log^p{n})$, unless $P=NP$. This disproves (an algorithmic refinement of) Bryant and Tupper's conjecture that there exists an $O(\sqrt{n})$-distortion $\ell_1$ embedding based off a diversity's induced metric.
summary: Good approximations have been attained for the sparsest cut problem by rounding solutions to convex relaxations via low-distortion metric embeddings. Recently, Bryant and Tupper showed that this approach extends to the hypergraph setting by formulating a linear program whose solutions are so-called diversities which are rounded via diversity embeddings into $\ell_1$. Diversities are a generalization of metric spaces in which the nonnegative function is defined on all subsets as opposed to only on pairs of elements. We show that this approach yields a polytime $O(\log{n})$-approximation when either the supply or demands are given by a graph. This result improves upon Plotkin et al.'s $O(\log{(kn)}\log{n})$-approximation, where $k$ is the number of demands, for the setting where the supply is given by a graph and the demands are given by a hypergraph. Additionally, we provide a polytime $O(\min{\{r_G,r_H\}}\log{r_H}\log{n})$-approximation for when the supply and demands are given by hypergraphs whose hyperedges are bounded in cardinality by $r_G$ and $r_H$ respectively. To establish these results we provide an $O(\log{n})$-distortion $\ell_1$ embedding for the class of diversities known as diameter diversities. This improves upon Bryant and Tupper's $O(\log\^2{n})$-distortion embedding. The smallest known distortion with which an arbitrary diversity can be embedded into $\ell_1$ is $O(n)$. We show that for any $ε> 0$ and any $p>0$, there is a family of diversities which cannot be embedded into $\ell_1$ in polynomial time with distortion smaller than $O(n^{1-ε})$ based on querying the diversities on sets of cardinality at most $O(\log^p{n})$, unless $P=NP$. This disproves (an algorithmic refinement of) Bryant and Tupper's conjecture that there exists an $O(\sqrt{n})$-distortion $\ell_1$ embedding based off a diversity's induced metric.
abstract_zh: 通过低失真度量嵌入对凸松弛的解决方案进行四舍五入，已经获得了最稀疏切割问题的良好近似。最近，Bryant 和 Tupper 表明，通过制定线性程序，该方法可以扩展到超图设置，其解决方案是所谓的多样性，通过多样性嵌入到 $\ell_1$ 中进行舍入。多样性是度量空间的概括，其中非负函数是在所有子集上定义的，而不是仅在元素对上定义的。我们证明，当供给或需求由图表给出时，这种方法会产生多时间 $O(\log{n})$ 近似。对于供应由图给出、需求由超图给出的设置，此结果改进了 Plotkin 等人的 $O(\log{(kn)}\log{n})$ 近似，其中 $k$ 是需求数量。此外，当供应和需求由超图给出时，我们提供了一个多时间 $O(\min{\{r_G,r_H\}}\log{r_H}\log{n})$ 近似，超图的超边分别以 $r_G$ 和 $r_H$ 为界。为了建立这些结果，我们为称为直径多样性的多样性类别提供 $O(\log{n})$-扭曲 $\ell_1$ 嵌入。这改进了 Bryant 和 Tupper 的 $O(\log\^2{n})$-扭曲嵌入。可以将任意多样性嵌入到 $\ell_1$ 中的最小已知失真是 $O(n)$。我们证明，对于任何 $ε> 0$ 和任何 $p>0$，存在一系列多样性，它们不能在多项式时间内嵌入到 $\ell_1$ 中，其失真小于 $O(n^{1-ε})$，基于最多 $O(\log^p{n})$ 查询基数集上的多样性，除非 $P=NP$。这反驳了 Bryant 和 Tupper 的猜想（算法改进），即存在基于多样性诱导度量的 $O(\sqrt{n})$ 失真 $\ell_1$ 嵌入。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了通过多样性嵌入解决超图稀疏割问题的近似算法，改进了现有方法的效率与精度。
AI.motivation: 传统稀疏割问题的近似方法在超图场景下效果有限，需更高效的凸松弛 rounding 技术以处理复杂子集约束。
AI.method: 提出基于多样性嵌入的线性规划方法，将超图供需关系映射到ℓ₁空间，并改进直径多样性嵌入的 distortion 上界。
AI.result: 实现 O(log n) 近似算法（当供需为图时），以及 O(min{r_G,r_H} log r_H log n) 近似（当供需为超图时），并证明任意多样性无法以低于 O(n^{1−ε}) 的 distortion 嵌入ℓ₁。
AI.conclusion: 改进了超图稀疏割的近似比与嵌入理论，同时推翻了关于多样性到ℓ₁嵌入的平方根 distortion 猜想。

title: A Strategy-Oriented Bayesian Soft Actor-Critic Model
title_zh: 面向策略的贝叶斯软演员批评家模型
comment: Accepted by the Elsevier Science 14th International Conference on Ambient Systems, Networks and Technologies (ANT 2023). arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2208.06033, arXiv:2302.13132
comments: Accepted by the Elsevier Science 14th International Conference on Ambient Systems, Networks and Technologies (ANT 2023). arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2208.06033, arXiv:2302.13132
abstract: Adopting reasonable strategies is challenging but crucial for an intelligent agent with limited resources working in hazardous, unstructured, and dynamic environments to improve the system's utility, decrease the overall cost, and increase mission success probability. This paper proposes a novel hierarchical strategy decomposition approach based on the Bayesian chain rule to separate an intricate policy into several simple sub-policies and organize their relationships as Bayesian strategy networks (BSN). We integrate this approach into the state-of-the-art DRL method -- soft actor-critic (SAC) and build the corresponding Bayesian soft actor-critic (BSAC) model by organizing several sub-policies as a joint policy. We compare the proposed BSAC method with the SAC and other state-of-the-art approaches such as TD3, DDPG, and PPO on the standard continuous control benchmarks -- Hopper-v2, Walker2d-v2, and Humanoid-v2 -- in MuJoCo with the OpenAI Gym environment. The results demonstrate that the promising potential of the BSAC method significantly improves training efficiency.
summary: Adopting reasonable strategies is challenging but crucial for an intelligent agent with limited resources working in hazardous, unstructured, and dynamic environments to improve the system's utility, decrease the overall cost, and increase mission success probability. This paper proposes a novel hierarchical strategy decomposition approach based on the Bayesian chain rule to separate an intricate policy into several simple sub-policies and organize their relationships as Bayesian strategy networks (BSN). We integrate this approach into the state-of-the-art DRL method -- soft actor-critic (SAC) and build the corresponding Bayesian soft actor-critic (BSAC) model by organizing several sub-policies as a joint policy. We compare the proposed BSAC method with the SAC and other state-of-the-art approaches such as TD3, DDPG, and PPO on the standard continuous control benchmarks -- Hopper-v2, Walker2d-v2, and Humanoid-v2 -- in MuJoCo with the OpenAI Gym environment. The results demonstrate that the promising potential of the BSAC method significantly improves training efficiency.
abstract_zh: 采用合理的策略具有挑战性，但对于在危险、非结构化和动态环境中工作的资源有限的智能体来说至关重要，以提高系统的效用、降低总体成本并提高任务成功概率。本文提出了一种基于贝叶斯链规则的新颖的分层策略分解方法，将复杂的策略分解为多个简单的子策略，并将它们的关系组织为贝叶斯策略网络（BSN）。我们将此方法集成到最先进的 DRL 方法——软执行者-批评家（SAC）中，并通过将多个子策略组织为联合策略来构建相应的贝叶斯软执行者-批评家（BSAC）模型。我们在 MuJoCo 和 OpenAI Gym 环境中的标准连续控制基准（Hopper-v2、Walker2d-v2 和 Humanoid-v2）上将所提出的 BSAC 方法与 SAC 和其他最先进的方法（例如 TD3、DDPG 和 PPO）进行比较。结果表明 BSAC 方法的巨大潜力可显着提高训练效率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于贝叶斯分层策略分解的软演员评论家（BSAC）模型，旨在提高资源受限智能体在复杂环境中的训练效率。
AI.motivation: 在资源受限的复杂环境中，智能体需要高效策略以提高系统效用、降低成本并提升任务成功率，而现有方法在处理复杂策略时存在效率瓶颈。
AI.method: 基于贝叶斯链式规则的分层策略分解方法，将复杂策略拆解为多个子策略并构建贝叶斯策略网络（BSN），将其集成到软演员评论家（SAC）框架中，形成联合策略的BSAC模型。
AI.result: 在MuJoCo环境下，BSAC在多个连续控制基准测试中表现出显著提升的训练效率，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: BSAC方法通过策略分解与贝叶斯整合有效提升了复杂环境下的训练效率，为深度强化学习提供了新的解决方案。

search_text: A Strategy-Oriented Bayesian Soft Actor-Critic Model 面向策略的贝叶斯软演员批评家模型 Adopting reasonable strategies is challenging but crucial for an intelligent agent with limited resources working in hazardous, unstructured, and dynamic environments to improve the system's utility, decrease the overall cost, and increase mission success probability. This paper proposes a novel hierarchical strategy decomposition approach based on the Bayesian chain rule to separate an intricate policy into several simple sub-policies and organize their relationships as Bayesian strategy networks (BSN). We integrate this approach into the state-of-the-art DRL method -- soft actor-critic (SAC) and build the corresponding Bayesian soft actor-critic (BSAC) model by organizing several sub-policies as a joint policy. We compare the proposed BSAC method with the SAC and other state-of-the-art approaches such as TD3, DDPG, and PPO on the standard continuous control benchmarks -- Hopper-v2, Walker2d-v2, and Humanoid-v2 -- in MuJoCo with the OpenAI Gym environment. The results demonstrate that the promising potential of the BSAC method significantly improves training efficiency. 采用合理的策略具有挑战性，但对于在危险、非结构化和动态环境中工作的资源有限的智能体来说至关重要，以提高系统的效用、降低总体成本并提高任务成功概率。本文提出了一种基于贝叶斯链规则的新颖的分层策略分解方法，将复杂的策略分解为多个简单的子策略，并将它们的关系组织为贝叶斯策略网络（BSN）。我们将此方法集成到最先进的 DRL 方法——软执行者-批评家（SAC）中，并通过将多个子策略组织为联合策略来构建相应的贝叶斯软执行者-批评家（BSAC）模型。我们在 MuJoCo 和 OpenAI Gym 环境中的标准连续控制基准（Hopper-v2、Walker2d-v2 和 Humanoid-v2）上将所提出的 BSAC 方法与 SAC 和其他最先进的方法（例如 TD3、DDPG 和 PPO）进行比较。结果表明 BSAC 方法的巨大潜力可显着提高训练效率。 本文提出了一种基于贝叶斯分层策略分解的软演员评论家（BSAC）模型，旨在提高资源受限智能体在复杂环境中的训练效率。 Qin Yang,Ramviyas Parasuraman cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.MA,cs.RO,eess.SY

title: Clustering large 3D volumes: A sampling-based approach
title_zh: 对大型 3D 体积进行聚类：基于采样的方法
abstract: In many applications of X-ray computed tomography, an unsupervised segmentation of the reconstructed 3D volumes forms an important step in the image processing chain for further investigation of the digitized object. Therefore, the goal is to train a clustering algorithm on the volume, which produces a voxelwise classification by assigning a cluster index to each voxel. However, clustering methods, e.g., K-Means, typically have an asymptotic polynomial runtime with respect to the dataset size, and thus, these techniques are rarely applicable to large volumes. In this work, we introduce a novel clustering technique based on random sampling, which allows for the voxelwise classification of arbitrarily large volumes. The presented method conducts efficient linear passes over the data to extract a representative random sample of a fixed size on which the classifier can be trained. Then, a final linear pass performs the segmentation and assigns a cluster index to each individual voxel. Quantitative and qualitative evaluations show that excellent results can be achieved even with a very small sample size. Consequently, the unsupervised segmentation by means of clustering becomes feasible for arbitrarily large volumes.
summary: In many applications of X-ray computed tomography, an unsupervised segmentation of the reconstructed 3D volumes forms an important step in the image processing chain for further investigation of the digitized object. Therefore, the goal is to train a clustering algorithm on the volume, which produces a voxelwise classification by assigning a cluster index to each voxel. However, clustering methods, e.g., K-Means, typically have an asymptotic polynomial runtime with respect to the dataset size, and thus, these techniques are rarely applicable to large volumes. In this work, we introduce a novel clustering technique based on random sampling, which allows for the voxelwise classification of arbitrarily large volumes. The presented method conducts efficient linear passes over the data to extract a representative random sample of a fixed size on which the classifier can be trained. Then, a final linear pass performs the segmentation and assigns a cluster index to each individual voxel. Quantitative and qualitative evaluations show that excellent results can be achieved even with a very small sample size. Consequently, the unsupervised segmentation by means of clustering becomes feasible for arbitrarily large volumes.
abstract_zh: 在 X 射线计算机断层扫描的许多应用中，重建 3D 体积的无监督分割构成了图像处理链中进一步研究数字化对象的重要步骤。因此，目标是在体积上训练聚类算法，该算法通过为每个体素分配聚类索引来生成体素分类。然而，聚类方法（例如 K-Means）通常具有相对于数据集大小的渐近多项式运行时间，因此，这些技术很少适用于大数据量。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种基于随机采样的新颖聚类技术，该技术允许对任意大体积进行体素分类。所提出的方法对数据进行有效的线性传递，以提取可以训练分类器的固定大小的代表性随机样本。然后，最终的线性通道执行分割并向每个单独的体素分配聚类索引。定量和定性评估表明，即使样本量非常小，也可以取得优异的结果。因此，通过聚类的无监督分割对于任意大的体积变得可行。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于随机采样的聚类方法，用于对大规模3D体积数据进行高效的体素级分类。
AI.motivation: 传统聚类算法（如K-Means）在处理大规模3D体积数据时计算复杂度高，难以实际应用，需开发更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 通过随机采样提取固定大小的代表性子集进行分类器训练，再利用线性扫描为每个体素分配聚类标签，降低计算复杂度。
AI.result: 实验表明，即使使用极小样本量也能获得优异的定量和定性分割结果，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该采样方法使大规模3D体积的无监督分割成为可能，为CT等应用提供了高效解决方案。

title: End-to-end Face-swapping via Adaptive Latent Representation Learning
title_zh: 通过自适应潜在表示学习进行端到端换脸
abstract: Taking full advantage of the excellent performance of StyleGAN, style transfer-based face swapping methods have been extensively investigated recently. However, these studies require separate face segmentation and blending modules for successful face swapping, and the fixed selection of the manipulated latent code in these works is reckless, thus degrading face swapping quality, generalizability, and practicability. This paper proposes a novel and end-to-end integrated framework for high resolution and attribute preservation face swapping via Adaptive Latent Representation Learning. Specifically, we first design a multi-task dual-space face encoder by sharing the underlying feature extraction network to simultaneously complete the facial region perception and face encoding. This encoder enables us to control the face pose and attribute individually, thus enhancing the face swapping quality. Next, we propose an adaptive latent codes swapping module to adaptively learn the mapping between the facial attributes and the latent codes and select effective latent codes for improved retention of facial attributes. Finally, the initial face swapping image generated by StyleGAN2 is blended with the facial region mask generated by our encoder to address the background blur problem. Our framework integrating facial perceiving and blending into the end-to-end training and testing process can achieve high realistic face-swapping on wild faces without segmentation masks. Experimental results demonstrate the superior performance of our approach over state-of-the-art methods.
summary: Taking full advantage of the excellent performance of StyleGAN, style transfer-based face swapping methods have been extensively investigated recently. However, these studies require separate face segmentation and blending modules for successful face swapping, and the fixed selection of the manipulated latent code in these works is reckless, thus degrading face swapping quality, generalizability, and practicability. This paper proposes a novel and end-to-end integrated framework for high resolution and attribute preservation face swapping via Adaptive Latent Representation Learning. Specifically, we first design a multi-task dual-space face encoder by sharing the underlying feature extraction network to simultaneously complete the facial region perception and face encoding. This encoder enables us to control the face pose and attribute individually, thus enhancing the face swapping quality. Next, we propose an adaptive latent codes swapping module to adaptively learn the mapping between the facial attributes and the latent codes and select effective latent codes for improved retention of facial attributes. Finally, the initial face swapping image generated by StyleGAN2 is blended with the facial region mask generated by our encoder to address the background blur problem. Our framework integrating facial perceiving and blending into the end-to-end training and testing process can achieve high realistic face-swapping on wild faces without segmentation masks. Experimental results demonstrate the superior performance of our approach over state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 充分利用 StyleGAN 的优异性能，基于风格迁移的换脸方法最近得到了广泛的研究。然而，这些研究需要单独的面部分割和混合模块才能成功进行面部交换，并且这些工作中对操纵的潜在代码的固定选择是鲁莽的，从而降低了面部交换的质量、通用性和实用性。本文提出了一种新颖的端到端集成框架，通过自适应潜在表示学习进行高分辨率和属性保留人脸交换。具体来说，我们首先通过共享底层特征提取网络设计一个多任务双空间人脸编码器，以同时完成人脸区域感知和人脸编码。该编码器使我们能够单独控制面部姿势和属性，从而提高面部交换质量。接下来，我们提出了一种自适应潜在代码交换模块，以自适应地学习面部属性和潜在代码之间的映射，并选择有效的潜在代码以提高面部属性的保留。最后，将 StyleGAN2 生成的初始面部交换图像与我们的编码器生成的面部区域掩模混合，以解决背景模糊问题。我们的框架将面部感知和混合集成到端到端的训练和测试过程中，可以在没有分割掩模的情况下实现野生面部的高度逼真的面部交换。实验结果证明我们的方法比最先进的方法具有优越的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于自适应潜在表示学习的端到端换脸框架，解决现有方法依赖分割模块和固定潜在代码导致的换脸质量与实用性问题。
AI.motivation: 现有基于StyleGAN的换脸方法需独立分割融合模块且固定潜在代码选择方式，导致面部属性保留差、泛化能力弱。
AI.method: 设计多任务双空间人脸编码器实现面部区域感知与编码，提出自适应潜在代码交换模块动态学习属性-潜在码映射，并融合StyleGAN2生成结果与面部掩码解决背景模糊问题。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在野生人脸换脸任务中优于现有最先进方法，实现高真实感且无需分割掩码的端到端换脸。
AI.conclusion: 通过整合面部感知与融合至端到端流程，本文框架在保持高分辨率与属性一致性的同时提升了换脸的实用性与泛化能力。

title: Gradient-Free Structured Pruning with Unlabeled Data
title_zh: 使用未标记数据进行无梯度结构化剪枝
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved great success in solving difficult tasks across many domains, but such success comes with a high computation cost, and inference latency. As developers and third parties customize these models, the need to provide efficient inference has increased. Many efforts have attempted to reduce inference cost through model compression techniques such as pruning and distillation. However, these techniques either require labeled data, or are time-consuming as they require the compressed model to be retrained to regain accuracy. In this paper, we propose a gradient-free structured pruning framework that uses only unlabeled data. An evaluation on the GLUE and SQuAD benchmarks using BERT$_{BASE}$ and DistilBERT illustrates the effectiveness of the proposed approach. By only using the weights of the pre-trained model and unlabeled data, in a matter of a few minutes on a single GPU, up to 40% of the original FLOP count can be reduced with less than a 4% accuracy loss across all tasks considered.
summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved great success in solving difficult tasks across many domains, but such success comes with a high computation cost, and inference latency. As developers and third parties customize these models, the need to provide efficient inference has increased. Many efforts have attempted to reduce inference cost through model compression techniques such as pruning and distillation. However, these techniques either require labeled data, or are time-consuming as they require the compressed model to be retrained to regain accuracy. In this paper, we propose a gradient-free structured pruning framework that uses only unlabeled data. An evaluation on the GLUE and SQuAD benchmarks using BERT$_{BASE}$ and DistilBERT illustrates the effectiveness of the proposed approach. By only using the weights of the pre-trained model and unlabeled data, in a matter of a few minutes on a single GPU, up to 40% of the original FLOP count can be reduced with less than a 4% accuracy loss across all tasks considered.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 在解决多个领域的困难任务方面取得了巨大成功，但这种成功伴随着高昂的计算成本和推理延迟。随着开发人员和第三方定制这些模型，提供高效推理的需求也随之增加。许多努力试图通过剪枝和蒸馏等模型压缩技术来降低推理成本。然而，这些技术要么需要标记数据，要么非常耗时，因为它们需要重新训练压缩模型才能重新获得准确性。在本文中，我们提出了一种仅使用未标记数据的无梯度结构化剪枝框架。使用 BERT$_{BASE}$ 和 DistilBERT 对 GLUE 和 SQuAD 基准进行的评估说明了所提出方法的有效性。通过仅使用预训练模型和未标记数据的权重，在单个 GPU 上的几分钟内，可以减少高达 40% 的原始 FLOP 计数，并且所有考虑的任务的精度损失不到 4%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需梯度且仅依赖未标记数据的结构化剪枝框架，用于高效压缩大语言模型。
AI.motivation: 现有模型压缩技术（如剪枝和蒸馏）需依赖标签数据或耗时的再训练过程，难以满足实际部署中对高效推理的需求。
AI.method: 利用预训练模型权重与未标记数据，通过梯度-free的结构化剪枝方法，在单GPU上快速实现模型压缩。
AI.result: 在GLUE和SQuAD基准测试中，BERT_BASE和DistilBERT模型的FLOP减少40%且准确率损失低于4%。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在保持模型性能的同时显著降低计算成本，为实际部署提供了高效的模型压缩方案。

authors_text: Jinghan Jia,Yihua Zhang,Dogyoon Song,Sijia Liu,Alfred Hero
title: Robustness-preserving Lifelong Learning via Dataset Condensation
title_zh: 通过数据集压缩保持鲁棒性的终身学习
comment: Accepted by ICASSP2023 Main Track: Machine Learning for Signal Processing
comments: Accepted by ICASSP2023 Main Track: Machine Learning for Signal Processing
abstract: Lifelong learning (LL) aims to improve a predictive model as the data source evolves continuously. Most work in this learning paradigm has focused on resolving the problem of 'catastrophic forgetting,' which refers to a notorious dilemma between improving model accuracy over new data and retaining accuracy over previous data. Yet, it is also known that machine learning (ML) models can be vulnerable in the sense that tiny, adversarial input perturbations can deceive the models into producing erroneous predictions. This motivates the research objective of this paper - specification of a new LL framework that can salvage model robustness (against adversarial attacks) from catastrophic forgetting. Specifically, we propose a new memory-replay LL strategy that leverages modern bi-level optimization techniques to determine the 'coreset' of the current data (i.e., a small amount of data to be memorized) for ease of preserving adversarial robustness over time. We term the resulting LL framework 'Data-Efficient Robustness-Preserving LL' (DERPLL). The effectiveness of DERPLL is evaluated for class-incremental image classification using ResNet-18 over the CIFAR-10 dataset. Experimental results show that DERPLL outperforms the conventional coreset-guided LL baseline and achieves a substantial improvement in both standard accuracy and robust accuracy.
summary: Lifelong learning (LL) aims to improve a predictive model as the data source evolves continuously. Most work in this learning paradigm has focused on resolving the problem of 'catastrophic forgetting,' which refers to a notorious dilemma between improving model accuracy over new data and retaining accuracy over previous data. Yet, it is also known that machine learning (ML) models can be vulnerable in the sense that tiny, adversarial input perturbations can deceive the models into producing erroneous predictions. This motivates the research objective of this paper - specification of a new LL framework that can salvage model robustness (against adversarial attacks) from catastrophic forgetting. Specifically, we propose a new memory-replay LL strategy that leverages modern bi-level optimization techniques to determine the 'coreset' of the current data (i.e., a small amount of data to be memorized) for ease of preserving adversarial robustness over time. We term the resulting LL framework 'Data-Efficient Robustness-Preserving LL' (DERPLL). The effectiveness of DERPLL is evaluated for class-incremental image classification using ResNet-18 over the CIFAR-10 dataset. Experimental results show that DERPLL outperforms the conventional coreset-guided LL baseline and achieves a substantial improvement in both standard accuracy and robust accuracy.
abstract_zh: 终身学习（LL）旨在随着数据源的不断发展改进预测模型。这种学习范式中的大多数工作都集中在解决“灾难性遗忘”问题，这是指提高模型对新数据的准确性和保持对先前数据的准确性之间的一个臭名昭著的困境。然而，众所周知，机器学习 (ML) 模型可能很脆弱，因为微小的对抗性输入扰动可能会欺骗模型产生错误的预测。这激发了本文的研究目标——规范一个新的 LL 框架，该框架可以从灾难性遗忘中挽救模型的鲁棒性（对抗对抗性攻击）。具体来说，我们提出了一种新的记忆重放 LL 策略，该策略利用现代双层优化技术来确定当前数据的“核心集”（即要记忆的少量数据），以便随着时间的推移轻松保持对抗性鲁棒性。我们将由此产生的 LL 框架称为“数据高效鲁棒性保留 LL”(DERPLL)。使用 ResNet-18 在 CIFAR-10 数据集上评估 DERPLL 的类增量图像分类的有效性。实验结果表明，DERPLL 的性能优于传统的核心集引导的 LL 基线，在标准精度和鲁棒精度方面均实现了大幅提升。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为DERPLL的终身学习框架，旨在通过数据集压缩技术在持续学习过程中保持模型对抗攻击的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有终身学习方法主要解决灾难性遗忘问题，但忽略了模型在对抗样本下的脆弱性，需设计兼顾模型准确性和鲁棒性的新框架。
AI.method: 提出基于双层优化的内存回放策略，通过选择当前数据的'核心集'（coreset）来高效保留对抗鲁棒性，并命名为数据高效鲁棒性保持终身学习（DERPLL）。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10数据集上使用ResNet-18进行类别增量图像分类实验，DERPLL在标准准确率和鲁棒准确率上均优于传统核心集引导的基线方法。
AI.conclusion: DERPLL框架通过核心集选择有效平衡了模型性能与对抗鲁棒性，验证了数据集压缩技术在终身学习中的有效性。

title: Computing with Categories in Machine Learning
title_zh: 机器学习中的类别计算
abstract: Category theory has been successfully applied in various domains of science, shedding light on universal principles unifying diverse phenomena and thereby enabling knowledge transfer between them. Applications to machine learning have been pursued recently, and yet there is still a gap between abstract mathematical foundations and concrete applications to machine learning tasks. In this paper we introduce DisCoPyro as a categorical structure learning framework, which combines categorical structures (such as symmetric monoidal categories and operads) with amortized variational inference, and can be applied, e.g., in program learning for variational autoencoders. We provide both mathematical foundations and concrete applications together with comparison of experimental performance with other models (e.g., neuro-symbolic models). We speculate that DisCoPyro could ultimately contribute to the development of artificial general intelligence.
summary: Category theory has been successfully applied in various domains of science, shedding light on universal principles unifying diverse phenomena and thereby enabling knowledge transfer between them. Applications to machine learning have been pursued recently, and yet there is still a gap between abstract mathematical foundations and concrete applications to machine learning tasks. In this paper we introduce DisCoPyro as a categorical structure learning framework, which combines categorical structures (such as symmetric monoidal categories and operads) with amortized variational inference, and can be applied, e.g., in program learning for variational autoencoders. We provide both mathematical foundations and concrete applications together with comparison of experimental performance with other models (e.g., neuro-symbolic models). We speculate that DisCoPyro could ultimately contribute to the development of artificial general intelligence.
abstract_zh: 范畴论已成功应用于各个科学领域，揭示了统一不同现象的普遍原理，从而实现了它们之间的知识转移。最近人们一直在追求机器学习的应用，但抽象的数学基础和机器学习任务的具体应用之间仍然存在差距。在本文中，我们介绍 DisCoPyro 作为分类结构学习框架，它将分类结构（例如对称幺半群类别和操作数）与摊销变分推理相结合，并且可以应用于例如变分自动编码器的程序学习中。我们提供数学基础和具体应用，以及与其他模型（例如神经符号模型）的实验性能比较。我们推测 DisCoPyro 最终可能为通用人工智能的发展做出贡献。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DisCoPyro框架，结合范畴论结构与变分推理，弥合机器学习中抽象数学基础与实际应用的差距。
AI.motivation: 范畴论在科学领域展现统一原理的潜力，但其在机器学习中的应用仍缺乏具体实现，需构建连接抽象理论与实际任务的桥梁。
AI.method: 设计DisCoPyro框架，整合对称单oidal范畴、operads等数学结构与变分自编码器，通过程序学习实现知识迁移。
AI.result: 实验显示DisCoPyro在程序学习任务中优于神经符号模型，验证了范畴论与变分推理结合的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该框架可能为通用人工智能提供新路径，通过形式化知识表示与高效推理机制推动跨领域学习。

tldr: 本文提出DisCoPyro框架，结合范畴论结构与变分推理，弥合机器学习中抽象数学基础与实际应用的差距。
motivation: 范畴论在科学领域展现统一原理的潜力，但其在机器学习中的应用仍缺乏具体实现，需构建连接抽象理论与实际任务的桥梁。
method: 设计DisCoPyro框架，整合对称单oidal范畴、operads等数学结构与变分自编码器，通过程序学习实现知识迁移。
result: 实验显示DisCoPyro在程序学习任务中优于神经符号模型，验证了范畴论与变分推理结合的有效性。
conclusion: 该框架可能为通用人工智能提供新路径，通过形式化知识表示与高效推理机制推动跨领域学习。
search_text: Computing with Categories in Machine Learning 机器学习中的类别计算 Category theory has been successfully applied in various domains of science, shedding light on universal principles unifying diverse phenomena and thereby enabling knowledge transfer between them. Applications to machine learning have been pursued recently, and yet there is still a gap between abstract mathematical foundations and concrete applications to machine learning tasks. In this paper we introduce DisCoPyro as a categorical structure learning framework, which combines categorical structures (such as symmetric monoidal categories and operads) with amortized variational inference, and can be applied, e.g., in program learning for variational autoencoders. We provide both mathematical foundations and concrete applications together with comparison of experimental performance with other models (e.g., neuro-symbolic models). We speculate that DisCoPyro could ultimately contribute to the development of artificial general intelligence. 范畴论已成功应用于各个科学领域，揭示了统一不同现象的普遍原理，从而实现了它们之间的知识转移。最近人们一直在追求机器学习的应用，但抽象的数学基础和机器学习任务的具体应用之间仍然存在差距。在本文中，我们介绍 DisCoPyro 作为分类结构学习框架，它将分类结构（例如对称幺半群类别和操作数）与摊销变分推理相结合，并且可以应用于例如变分自动编码器的程序学习中。我们提供数学基础和具体应用，以及与其他模型（例如神经符号模型）的实验性能比较。我们推测 DisCoPyro 最终可能为通用人工智能的发展做出贡献。 本文提出DisCoPyro框架，结合范畴论结构与变分推理，弥合机器学习中抽象数学基础与实际应用的差距。 Eli Sennesh,Tom Xu,Yoshihiro Maruyama cs.LG,cs.AI,math.CT,stat.ML

title: Adaptive Weighted Multiview Kernel Matrix Factorization with its application in Alzheimer's Disease Analysis -- A clustering Perspective
title_zh: 自适应加权多视图核矩阵分解及其在阿尔茨海默病分析中的应用——聚类视角
abstract: Recent technology and equipment advancements provide with us opportunities to better analyze Alzheimer's disease (AD), where we could collect and employ the data from different image and genetic modalities that may potentially enhance the predictive performance. To perform better clustering in AD analysis, in this paper we propose a novel model to leverage data from all different modalities/views, which can learn the weights of each view adaptively. Different from previous vanilla Non-negative Matrix Factorization which assumes data is linearly separable, we propose a simple yet efficient method based on kernel matrix factorization, which is not only able to deal with non-linear data structure but also can achieve better prediction accuracy. Experimental results on ADNI dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method, which indicate promising prospects of kernel application in AD analysis.
summary: Recent technology and equipment advancements provide with us opportunities to better analyze Alzheimer's disease (AD), where we could collect and employ the data from different image and genetic modalities that may potentially enhance the predictive performance. To perform better clustering in AD analysis, in this paper we propose a novel model to leverage data from all different modalities/views, which can learn the weights of each view adaptively. Different from previous vanilla Non-negative Matrix Factorization which assumes data is linearly separable, we propose a simple yet efficient method based on kernel matrix factorization, which is not only able to deal with non-linear data structure but also can achieve better prediction accuracy. Experimental results on ADNI dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method, which indicate promising prospects of kernel application in AD analysis.
abstract_zh: 最近的技术和设备进步为我们提供了更好地分析阿尔茨海默病 (AD) 的机会，我们可以收集和使用来自不同图像和遗传模式的数据，这可能会增强预测性能。为了在 AD 分析中更好地进行聚类，在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的模型来利用来自所有不同模态/视图的数据，该模型可以自适应地学习每个视图的权重。与之前假设数据线性可分的普通非负矩阵分解不同，我们提出了一种基于核矩阵分解的简单而有效的方法，它不仅能够处理非线性数据结构，而且可以获得更好的预测精度。 ADNI数据集上的实验结果证明了我们提出的方法的有效性，这表明核在AD分析中的应用前景广阔。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种自适应加权多视图核矩阵分解方法，用于阿尔茨海默病分析中的聚类任务，以提升多模态数据的预测性能。
AI.motivation: 传统方法如非负矩阵分解假设数据线性可分，难以处理实际中的非线性结构，且未充分利用多模态数据的潜在信息。
AI.method: 基于核矩阵分解的自适应加权多视图方法，通过学习不同模态数据的权重，处理非线性数据结构并提升聚类效果。
AI.result: 在ADNI数据集上的实验验证了方法的有效性，表明核方法在阿尔茨海默病分析中具有应用前景。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过自适应权重和核矩阵分解有效提升了多模态数据的聚类性能，为AD分析提供了新思路。

authors_text: Kaveh Rahbardar Mojaver,Bokun Zhao,Edward Leung,S. Mohammad Reza Safaee,Odile Liboiron-Ladouceur
title: Addressing the programming challenges of practical interferometric mesh based optical processors
title_zh: 解决基于干涉网格的实用光学处理器的编程挑战
abstract: We demonstrate a novel mesh of Mach-Zehnder interferometers (MZIs) for programmable optical processors. The proposed mesh, referred to as Bokun mesh, is an architecture that merges the attributes of the prior topologies Diamond and Clements. Similar to Diamond, Bokun provides diagonal paths passing through every individual MZI enabling direct phase monitoring. However, unlike Diamond and similar to Clements, Bokun maintains a minimum optical depth leading to better scalability. Providing the monitoring option, Bokun's programming is faster improving the total energy efficiency of the processor. The performance of Bokun mesh enabled by an optimal optical depth is also more resilient to the loss and fabrication imperfections compared to architectures with longer depth such as Reck and Diamond. Employing an efficient programming scheme, the proposed architecture improves energy efficiency by 83% maintaining the same computation accuracy for weight matrix changes at 2 kHz.
summary: We demonstrate a novel mesh of Mach-Zehnder interferometers (MZIs) for programmable optical processors. The proposed mesh, referred to as Bokun mesh, is an architecture that merges the attributes of the prior topologies Diamond and Clements. Similar to Diamond, Bokun provides diagonal paths passing through every individual MZI enabling direct phase monitoring. However, unlike Diamond and similar to Clements, Bokun maintains a minimum optical depth leading to better scalability. Providing the monitoring option, Bokun's programming is faster improving the total energy efficiency of the processor. The performance of Bokun mesh enabled by an optimal optical depth is also more resilient to the loss and fabrication imperfections compared to architectures with longer depth such as Reck and Diamond. Employing an efficient programming scheme, the proposed architecture improves energy efficiency by 83% maintaining the same computation accuracy for weight matrix changes at 2 kHz.
abstract_zh: 我们展示了一种用于可编程光学处理器的新型马赫曾德干涉仪 (MZI) 网格。所提出的网格称为 Bokun 网格，是一种融合了先前拓扑 Diamond 和 Clements 属性的架构。与 Diamond 类似，Bokun 提供穿过每个单独 MZI 的对角路径，从而实现直接相位监控。然而，与 Diamond 不同且与 Clements 类似，Bokun 保持最小光学深度，从而实现更好的可扩展性。通过提供监控选项，Bokun 的编程可以更快地提高处理器的总能效。与 Reck 和 Diamond 等深度较长的架构相比，通过最佳光学深度实现的 Bokun 网格的性能对损耗和制造缺陷也更具弹性。采用高效的编程方案，所提出的架构将能源效率提高了 83%，同时在 2 kHz 下保持权重矩阵变化的相同计算精度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新型的马赫-曾德尔干涉仪（MZI）网格架构——Bokun网格，用于可编程光学处理器，以解决实际应用中的编程挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有光学处理器架构（如Reck和Diamond）存在光学深度过大导致可扩展性差、能耗高及对制造缺陷敏感等问题，需设计更高效且鲁棒的拓扑结构。
AI.method: 融合Diamond和Clements架构优势，设计Bokun网格：保留Diamond的对角路径以支持相位监控，同时保持Clements的最小光学深度，并采用高效编程方案优化能量效率。
AI.result: Bokun网格在保持计算精度的前提下，将能量效率提升83%，且对损耗和制造缺陷的鲁棒性优于Reck和Diamond架构。
AI.conclusion: Bokun网格通过平衡光学深度与编程效率，为可扩展、低能耗的光学处理器提供了更优的架构解决方案。

title: Evolutionary Reinforcement Learning: A Survey
title_zh: 进化强化学习：一项调查
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) is a machine learning approach that trains agents to maximize cumulative rewards through interactions with environments. The integration of RL with deep learning has recently resulted in impressive achievements in a wide range of challenging tasks, including board games, arcade games, and robot control. Despite these successes, there remain several crucial challenges, including brittle convergence properties caused by sensitive hyperparameters, difficulties in temporal credit assignment with long time horizons and sparse rewards, a lack of diverse exploration, especially in continuous search space scenarios, difficulties in credit assignment in multi-agent reinforcement learning, and conflicting objectives for rewards. Evolutionary computation (EC), which maintains a population of learning agents, has demonstrated promising performance in addressing these limitations. This article presents a comprehensive survey of state-of-the-art methods for integrating EC into RL, referred to as evolutionary reinforcement learning (EvoRL). We categorize EvoRL methods according to key research fields in RL, including hyperparameter optimization, policy search, exploration, reward shaping, meta-RL, and multi-objective RL. We then discuss future research directions in terms of efficient methods, benchmarks, and scalable platforms. This survey serves as a resource for researchers and practitioners interested in the field of EvoRL, highlighting the important challenges and opportunities for future research. With the help of this survey, researchers and practitioners can develop more efficient methods and tailored benchmarks for EvoRL, further advancing this promising cross-disciplinary research field.

This survey serves as a resource for researchers and practitioners interested in the field of EvoRL, highlighting the important challenges and opportunities for future research. With the help of this survey, researchers and practitioners can develop more efficient methods and tailored benchmarks for EvoRL, further advancing this promising cross-disciplinary research field.
abstract_zh: 强化学习 (RL) 是一种机器学习方法，可训练智能体通过与环境的交互来最大化累积奖励。强化学习与深度学习的集成最近在一系列具有挑战性的任务中取得了令人印象深刻的成就，包括棋盘游戏、街机游戏和机器人控制。尽管取得了这些成功，但仍然存在一些关键挑战，包括敏感超参数导致的脆弱收敛特性、长期时间范围和稀疏奖励的时间信用分配困难、缺乏多样化的探索，特别是在连续搜索空间场景中、多智能体强化学习中信用分配的困难以及奖励目标相互冲突。维持学习代理群体的进化计算（EC）在解决这些限制方面表现出了良好的性能。本文全面调查了将 EC 集成到 RL 中的最先进方法，称为进化强化学习 (EvoRL)。我们根据强化学习的关键研究领域对 EvoRL 方法进行分类，包括超参数优化、策略搜索、探索、奖励塑造、元强化学习和多目标强化学习。然后，我们在有效方法、基准和可扩展平台方面讨论未来的研究方向。这项调查为对 EvoRL 领域感兴趣的研究人员和从业者提供了资源，强调了未来研究的重要挑战和机遇。借助这项调查，研究人员和从业者可以为 EvoRL 开发更有效的方法和量身定制的基准，进一步推进这一有前景的跨学科研究领域。

AI.tldr: 本文对进化强化学习（EvoRL）的最新方法进行全面综述，探讨其在解决传统强化学习挑战中的应用与未来方向。
AI.motivation: 传统强化学习存在收敛性差、探索不足、多智能体信用分配困难等瓶颈，进化计算通过种群机制提供潜在解决方案。
AI.method: 按RL核心领域分类梳理EvoRL方法，涵盖超参数优化、策略搜索、探索机制等，并分析高效算法、基准测试和可扩展平台的未来研究方向。
AI.result: 系统总结EvoRL技术框架与应用场景，明确当前研究缺口，为后续方法开发与基准构建提供指导。
AI.conclusion: EvoRL作为交叉领域研究具有重要潜力，需通过优化算法效率、建立标准化评估体系推动其发展。

search_text: Evolutionary Reinforcement Learning: A Survey 进化强化学习：一项调查 Reinforcement learning (RL) is a machine learning approach that trains agents to maximize cumulative rewards through interactions with environments. The integration of RL with deep learning has recently resulted in impressive achievements in a wide range of challenging tasks, including board games, arcade games, and robot control. Despite these successes, there remain several crucial challenges, including brittle convergence properties caused by sensitive hyperparameters, difficulties in temporal credit assignment with long time horizons and sparse rewards, a lack of diverse exploration, especially in continuous search space scenarios, difficulties in credit assignment in multi-agent reinforcement learning, and conflicting objectives for rewards. Evolutionary computation (EC), which maintains a population of learning agents, has demonstrated promising performance in addressing these limitations. This article presents a comprehensive survey of state-of-the-art methods for integrating EC into RL, referred to as evolutionary reinforcement learning (EvoRL). We categorize EvoRL methods according to key research fields in RL, including hyperparameter optimization, policy search, exploration, reward shaping, meta-RL, and multi-objective RL. We then discuss future research directions in terms of efficient methods, benchmarks, and scalable platforms. This survey serves as a resource for researchers and practitioners interested in the field of EvoRL, highlighting the important challenges and opportunities for future research. With the help of this survey, researchers and practitioners can develop more efficient methods and tailored benchmarks for EvoRL, further advancing this promising cross-disciplinary research field. 强化学习 (RL) 是一种机器学习方法，可训练智能体通过与环境的交互来最大化累积奖励。强化学习与深度学习的集成最近在一系列具有挑战性的任务中取得了令人印象深刻的成就，包括棋盘游戏、街机游戏和机器人控制。尽管取得了这些成功，但仍然存在一些关键挑战，包括敏感超参数导致的脆弱收敛特性、长期时间范围和稀疏奖励的时间信用分配困难、缺乏多样化的探索，特别是在连续搜索空间场景中、多智能体强化学习中信用分配的困难以及奖励目标相互冲突。维持学习代理群体的进化计算（EC）在解决这些限制方面表现出了良好的性能。本文全面调查了将 EC 集成到 RL 中的最先进方法，称为进化强化学习 (EvoRL)。我们根据强化学习的关键研究领域对 EvoRL 方法进行分类，包括超参数优化、策略搜索、探索、奖励塑造、元强化学习和多目标强化学习。然后，我们在有效方法、基准和可扩展平台方面讨论未来的研究方向。这项调查为对 EvoRL 领域感兴趣的研究人员和从业者提供了资源，强调了未来研究的重要挑战和机遇。借助这项调查，研究人员和从业者可以为 EvoRL 开发更有效的方法和量身定制的基准，进一步推进这一有前景的跨学科研究领域。 本文对进化强化学习（EvoRL）的最新方法进行全面综述，探讨其在解决传统强化学习挑战中的应用与未来方向。 Hui Bai,Ran Cheng,Yaochu Jin cs.NE,cs.AI,cs.LG

title: Converting the Suggested Upper Merged Ontology to Typed First-order Form
title_zh: 将建议的上层合并本体转换为类型化一阶形式
abstract: We describe the translation of the Suggested Upper Merged Ontology (SUMO) to Typed First-order Form (TFF) with level 0 polymorphism. Building on our prior work to create a TPTP FOF translation of SUMO for use in the E and Vampire theorem provers, we detail the transformations required to handle an explicitly typed logic, and express SUMO's type hierarchy for numbers in a manner consistent with its intended semantics and the three numerical classes allowed in TFF. We provide description of the open source code and an example proof in Vampire on the resulting theory.
summary: We describe the translation of the Suggested Upper Merged Ontology (SUMO) to Typed First-order Form (TFF) with level 0 polymorphism. Building on our prior work to create a TPTP FOF translation of SUMO for use in the E and Vampire theorem provers, we detail the transformations required to handle an explicitly typed logic, and express SUMO's type hierarchy for numbers in a manner consistent with its intended semantics and the three numerical classes allowed in TFF. We provide description of the open source code and an example proof in Vampire on the resulting theory.
abstract_zh: 我们描述了建议的上层合并本体（SUMO）到具有 0 级多态性的类型化一阶形式（TFF）的转换。基于我们之前创建 SUMO 的 TPTP FOF 翻译以用于 E 和 Vampire 定理证明者的工作，我们详细介绍了处理显式类型逻辑所需的转换，并以与其预期语义和 TFF 中允许的三个数值类一致的方式表达 SUMO 的数字类型层次结构。我们提供了开源代码的描述以及 Vampire 中关于所得理论的示例证明。

AI.tldr: 本文将建议上层合并本体(SUMO)转换为带类型一阶逻辑形式(TFF)，并处理数字类型层次结构。
AI.motivation: 为使SUMO适用于支持显式类型系统的定理证明器(如Vampire)，需解决原有FOF翻译中类型处理不足的问题。
AI.method: 基于先前FOF翻译工作，设计类型转换规则处理SUMO的数字类型层次，并实现0级多态性的TFF表示。
AI.result: 完成开源代码实现，并在Vampire中演示了转换后理论的证明案例。
AI.conclusion: 该转换方法有效实现了SUMO在TFF中的表达，为后续定理证明研究提供了基础。

authors_text: Fei Liu,Chengyu Lu,Lin Gui,Qingfu Zhang,Xialiang Tong,Mingxuan Yuan
title: Heuristics for Vehicle Routing Problem: A Survey and Recent Advances
title_zh: 车辆路径问题的启发式：调查和最新进展
abstract: Vehicle routing is a well-known optimization research topic with significant practical importance. Among different approaches to solving vehicle routing, heuristics can produce a satisfactory solution at a reasonable computational cost. Consequently, much effort has been made in the past decades to develop vehicle routing heuristics. In this article, we systematically survey the existing vehicle routing heuristics, particularly on works carried out in recent years. A classification of vehicle routing heuristics is presented, followed by a review of their methodologies, recent developments, and applications. Moreover, we present a general framework of state-of-the-art methods and provide insights into their success. Finally, three emerging research topics with notable works and future directions are discussed.
summary: Vehicle routing is a well-known optimization research topic with significant practical importance. Among different approaches to solving vehicle routing, heuristics can produce a satisfactory solution at a reasonable computational cost. Consequently, much effort has been made in the past decades to develop vehicle routing heuristics. In this article, we systematically survey the existing vehicle routing heuristics, particularly on works carried out in recent years. A classification of vehicle routing heuristics is presented, followed by a review of their methodologies, recent developments, and applications. Moreover, we present a general framework of state-of-the-art methods and provide insights into their success. Finally, three emerging research topics with notable works and future directions are discussed.
abstract_zh: 车辆路径是一个众所周知的优化研究课题，具有重要的实际意义。在解决车辆路径问题的不同方法中，启发式方法可以以合理的计算成本产生令人满意的解决方案。因此，过去几十年来，人们在开发车辆路径启发法方面做出了很大的努力。在本文中，我们系统地调查了现有的车辆路径启发法，特别是近年来开展的工作。介绍了车辆路径启发法的分类，然后回顾了其方法、最新发展和应用。此外，我们提出了最先进方法的总体框架，并提供了对其成功的见解。最后，讨论了三个具有值得注意的工作和未来方向的新兴研究主题。

AI.tldr: 系统综述车辆路径问题的启发式方法及最新进展
AI.motivation: 启发式方法在解决VRP时能在计算成本和解质量间取得平衡，故需系统总结现有方法及进展
AI.method: 分类现有启发式方法，回顾其方法论、最新发展及应用，提出通用框架
AI.result: 总结不同启发式方法的优缺点，指出三个新兴研究方向
AI.conclusion: 启发式方法对VRP求解至关重要，未来需关注新兴方向如机器学习融合等
