title: Can We Scale Transformers to Predict Parameters of Diverse ImageNet Models?
title_zh: 我们可以扩展 Transformer 来预测不同 ImageNet 模型的参数吗？
comment: ICML 2023, camera ready (7 tables with extra results added), code and models are at https://github.com/SamsungSAILMontreal/ghn3
comments: ICML 2023, camera ready (7 tables with extra results added), code and models are at https://github.com/SamsungSAILMontreal/ghn3
abstract: Pretraining a neural network on a large dataset is becoming a cornerstone in machine learning that is within the reach of only a few communities with large-resources. We aim at an ambitious goal of democratizing pretraining. Towards that goal, we train and release a single neural network that can predict high quality ImageNet parameters of other neural networks. By using predicted parameters for initialization we are able to boost training of diverse ImageNet models available in PyTorch. When transferred to other datasets, models initialized with predicted parameters also converge faster and reach competitive final performance.
summary: Pretraining a neural network on a large dataset is becoming a cornerstone in machine learning that is within the reach of only a few communities with large-resources. We aim at an ambitious goal of democratizing pretraining. Towards that goal, we train and release a single neural network that can predict high quality ImageNet parameters of other neural networks. By using predicted parameters for initialization we are able to boost training of diverse ImageNet models available in PyTorch. When transferred to other datasets, models initialized with predicted parameters also converge faster and reach competitive final performance.
abstract_zh: 在大型数据集上预训练神经网络正在成为机器学习的基石，只有少数拥有大量资源的社区才能做到这一点。我们的目标是实现预训练民主化的宏伟目标。为了实现这一目标，我们训练并发布了一个可以预测其他神经网络的高质量 ImageNet 参数的神经网络。通过使用预测参数进行初始化，我们能够增强 PyTorch 中可用的各种 ImageNet 模型的训练。当转移到其他数据集时，使用预测参数初始化的模型也会更快地收敛并达到有竞争力的最终性能。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出使用Transformer模型预测多种ImageNet模型的参数，以加速训练过程并提升模型性能。
AI.motivation: 预训练大型神经网络需要大量资源，仅少数社区可实现，论文旨在通过预测参数降低资源门槛，推动预训练民主化。
AI.method: 训练一个Transformer模型预测其他神经网络的ImageNet参数，并将预测参数作为初始化值用于PyTorch中的多样化模型训练。
AI.result: 使用预测参数初始化后，模型训练速度加快，最终性能达到竞争水平；在其他数据集上也表现出更快收敛和良好性能。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效实现了参数预测，证明了预训练模型初始化的可行性，为资源有限的研究者提供了实用工具。

search_text: Can We Scale Transformers to Predict Parameters of Diverse ImageNet Models? 我们可以扩展 Transformer 来预测不同 ImageNet 模型的参数吗？ Pretraining a neural network on a large dataset is becoming a cornerstone in machine learning that is within the reach of only a few communities with large-resources. We aim at an ambitious goal of democratizing pretraining. Towards that goal, we train and release a single neural network that can predict high quality ImageNet parameters of other neural networks. By using predicted parameters for initialization we are able to boost training of diverse ImageNet models available in PyTorch. When transferred to other datasets, models initialized with predicted parameters also converge faster and reach competitive final performance. 在大型数据集上预训练神经网络正在成为机器学习的基石，只有少数拥有大量资源的社区才能做到这一点。我们的目标是实现预训练民主化的宏伟目标。为了实现这一目标，我们训练并发布了一个可以预测其他神经网络的高质量 ImageNet 参数的神经网络。通过使用预测参数进行初始化，我们能够增强 PyTorch 中可用的各种 ImageNet 模型的训练。当转移到其他数据集时，使用预测参数初始化的模型也会更快地收敛并达到有竞争力的最终性能。 该论文提出使用Transformer模型预测多种ImageNet模型的参数，以加速训练过程并提升模型性能。 Boris Knyazev,Doha Hwang,Simon Lacoste-Julien cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CV,stat.ML

title: From Copilot to Pilot: Towards AI Supported Software Development
title_zh: 从副驾驶到飞行员：迈向人工智能支持的软件开发
abstract: AI-supported programming has arrived, as shown by the introduction and successes of large language models for code, such as Copilot/Codex (Github/OpenAI) and AlphaCode (DeepMind). Above human average performance on programming challenges is now possible. However, software engineering is much more than solving programming contests. Moving beyond code completion to AI-supported software engineering will require an AI system that can, among other things, understand how to avoid code smells, to follow language idioms, and eventually (maybe!) propose rational software designs. In this study, we explore the current limitations of AI-supported code completion tools like Copilot and offer a simple taxonomy for understanding the classification of AI-supported code completion tools in this space. We first perform an exploratory study on Copilot's code suggestions for language idioms and code smells. Copilot does not follow language idioms and avoid code smells in most of our test scenarios. We then conduct additional investigation to determine the current boundaries of AI-supported code completion tools like Copilot by introducing a taxonomy of software abstraction hierarchies where 'basic programming functionality' such as code compilation and syntax checking is at the least abstract level, software architecture analysis and design are at the most abstract level. We conclude by providing a discussion on challenges for future development of AI-supported code completion tools to reach the design level of abstraction in our taxonomy.
summary: AI-supported programming has arrived, as shown by the introduction and successes of large language models for code, such as Copilot/Codex (Github/OpenAI) and AlphaCode (DeepMind). Above human average performance on programming challenges is now possible. However, software engineering is much more than solving programming contests. Moving beyond code completion to AI-supported software engineering will require an AI system that can, among other things, understand how to avoid code smells, to follow language idioms, and eventually (maybe!) propose rational software designs. In this study, we explore the current limitations of AI-supported code completion tools like Copilot and offer a simple taxonomy for understanding the classification of AI-supported code completion tools in this space. We first perform an exploratory study on Copilot's code suggestions for language idioms and code smells. Copilot does not follow language idioms and avoid code smells in most of our test scenarios. We then conduct additional investigation to determine the current boundaries of AI-supported code completion tools like Copilot by introducing a taxonomy of software abstraction hierarchies where 'basic programming functionality' such as code compilation and syntax checking is at the least abstract level, software architecture analysis and design are at the most abstract level. We conclude by providing a discussion on challenges for future development of AI-supported code completion tools to reach the design level of abstraction in our taxonomy.
abstract_zh: AI 支持的编程已经到来，大型代码语言模型的引入和成功就证明了这一点，例如 Copilot/Codex (Github/OpenAI) 和 AlphaCode (DeepMind)。现在可以在编程挑战中取得高于人类平均水平的表现。然而，软件工程不仅仅是解决编程竞赛。从代码补全转向人工智能支持的软件工程将需要一个人工智能系统，除其他外，该系统可以理解如何避免代码异味、遵循语言习惯用法，并最终（也许！）提出合理的软件设计。在本研究中，我们探讨了 Copilot 等人工智能支持的代码完成工具当前的局限性，并提供了一个简单的分类法来理解该领域人工智能支持的代码完成工具的分类。我们首先对 Copilot 针对语言习语和代码味道的代码建议进行了探索性研究。在我们的大多数测试场景中，Copilot 不遵循语言惯用法并避免代码异味。然后，我们进行额外的调查，通过引入软件抽象层次结构的分类法来确定人工智能支持的代码完成工具（如 Copilot）的当前边界，其中代码编译和语法检查等“基本编程功能”处于最低抽象级别，软件架构分析和设计处于最抽象级别。最后，我们讨论了人工智能支持的代码完成工具未来开发所面临的挑战，以达到分类法中的抽象设计级别。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了AI辅助编程工具（如Copilot）在软件工程中的应用现状及其局限性，并提出了一种软件抽象层次的分类方法。
AI.motivation: 尽管AI辅助编程工具在解决编程挑战方面表现出色，但在软件工程的更广泛领域（如避免代码异味、遵循语言习惯和提出合理软件设计）仍存在明显不足，需要进一步研究其局限性及发展方向。
AI.method: 通过探索性研究分析Copilot在语言习惯和代码异味方面的代码建议，同时引入软件抽象层次分类法，评估AI辅助工具在不同抽象层级上的能力。
AI.result: Copilot在大多数测试场景中未能遵循语言习惯或避免代码异味，并且其能力主要集中在基础编程功能层面，难以达到软件架构分析和设计等较高抽象层级。
AI.conclusion: AI辅助编程工具要达到软件设计层面，还需克服诸多挑战，未来研究应聚焦于提升工具在抽象层次上的能力，以更好地支持软件工程实践。

authors_text: Katarina Doctor,Christine Task,Eric Kildebeck,Mayank Kejriwal,Lawrence Holder,Russell Leong
title: Toward Defining a Domain Complexity Measure Across Domains
title_zh: 跨域定义域复杂性度量
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems planned for deployment in real-world applications frequently are researched and developed in closed simulation environments where all variables are controlled and known to the simulator or labeled benchmark datasets are used. Transition from these simulators, testbeds, and benchmark datasets to more open-world domains poses significant challenges to AI systems, including significant increases in the complexity of the domain and the inclusion of real-world novelties; the open-world environment contains numerous out-of-distribution elements that are not part in the AI systems' training set. Here, we propose a path to a general, domain-independent measure of domain complexity level. We distinguish two aspects of domain complexity: intrinsic and extrinsic. The intrinsic domain complexity is the complexity that exists by itself without any action or interaction from an AI agent performing a task on that domain. This is an agent-independent aspect of the domain complexity. The extrinsic domain complexity is agent- and task-dependent. Intrinsic and extrinsic elements combined capture the overall complexity of the domain. We frame the components that define and impact domain complexity levels in a domain-independent light. Domain-independent measures of complexity could enable quantitative predictions of the difficulty posed to AI systems when transitioning from one testbed or environment to another, when facing out-of-distribution data in open-world tasks, and when navigating the rapidly expanding solution and search spaces encountered in open-world domains.
summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems planned for deployment in real-world applications frequently are researched and developed in closed simulation environments where all variables are controlled and known to the simulator or labeled benchmark datasets are used. Transition from these simulators, testbeds, and benchmark datasets to more open-world domains poses significant challenges to AI systems, including significant increases in the complexity of the domain and the inclusion of real-world novelties; the open-world environment contains numerous out-of-distribution elements that are not part in the AI systems' training set. The intrinsic domain complexity is the complexity that exists by itself without any action or interaction from an AI agent performing a task on that domain. This is an agent-independent aspect of the domain complexity. The extrinsic domain complexity is agent- and task-dependent. Intrinsic and extrinsic elements combined capture the overall complexity of the domain. We frame the components that define and impact domain complexity levels in a domain-independent light. Domain-independent measures of complexity could enable quantitative predictions of the difficulty posed to AI systems when transitioning from one testbed or environment to another, when facing out-of-distribution data in open-world tasks, and when navigating the rapidly expanding solution and search spaces encountered in open-world domains.
abstract_zh: 计划在现实世界应用中部署的人工智能（AI）系统经常在封闭的模拟环境中进行研究和开发，其中所有变量都受到控制并为模拟器所知，或者使用标记的基准数据集。从这些模拟器、测试床和基准数据集到更加开放的领域的过渡给人工智能系统带来了重大挑战，包括领域复杂性的显着增加以及现实世界新奇事物的纳入；开放世界环境包含许多不属于人工智能系统训练集的分布外元素。在这里，我们提出了一种通用的、独立于领域的领域复杂性级别度量的路径。我们区分领域复杂性的两个方面：内在的和外在的。内在域复杂性是指在没有人工智能代理在该域上执行任务的任何操作或交互的情况下本身存在的复杂性。这是域复杂性的一个独立于代理的方面。外在域复杂性取决于代理和任务。内在和外在元素的结合体现了该领域的整体复杂性。我们以独立于域的角度构建定义和影响域复杂性级别的组件。与领域无关的复杂性测量可以定量预测人工智能系统从一个测试平台或环境过渡到另一个测试平台或环境时、在开放世界任务中面对分布外数据时、以及在开放世界域中遇到的快速扩展的解决方案和搜索空间时所面临的困难。

AI.tldr: 提出一种跨领域的复杂度度量方法，以量化AI系统在开放世界中的挑战
AI.motivation: AI系统在迁移到开放世界时面临领域复杂性和分布外元素的挑战，现有方法缺乏通用的复杂度评估框架
AI.method: 区分领域复杂度的内在（领域固有属性）和外在（代理/任务依赖）维度，构建领域无关的复杂度分析框架
AI.result: 建立了理论框架用于解析领域复杂度构成要素，为复杂度量化提供基础
AI.conclusion: 该度量方法可预测AI系统在跨环境迁移时的难度，促进更鲁棒的系统设计

ai_content.motivation: AI系统在迁移到开放世界时面临领域复杂性和分布外元素的挑战，现有方法缺乏通用的复杂度评估框架

search_text: Toward Defining a Domain Complexity Measure Across Domains 跨域定义域复杂性度量 Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems planned for deployment in real-world applications frequently are researched and developed in closed simulation environments where all variables are controlled and known to the simulator or labeled benchmark datasets are used. Transition from these simulators, testbeds, and benchmark datasets to more open-world domains poses significant challenges to AI systems, including significant increases in the complexity of the domain and the inclusion of real-world novelties; the open-world environment contains numerous out-of-distribution elements that are not part in the AI systems' training set. Here, we propose a path to a general, domain-independent measure of domain complexity level. We distinguish two aspects of domain complexity: intrinsic and extrinsic. The intrinsic domain complexity is the complexity that exists by itself without any action or interaction from an AI agent performing a task on that domain. This is an agent-independent aspect of the domain complexity. The extrinsic domain complexity is agent- and task-dependent. Intrinsic and extrinsic elements combined capture the overall complexity of the domain. We frame the components that define and impact domain complexity levels in a domain-independent light. Domain-independent measures of complexity could enable quantitative predictions of the difficulty posed to AI systems when transitioning from one testbed or environment to another, when facing out-of-distribution data in open-world tasks, and when navigating the rapidly expanding solution and search spaces encountered in open-world domains. 计划在现实世界应用中部署的人工智能（AI）系统经常在封闭的模拟环境中进行研究和开发，其中所有变量都受到控制并为模拟器所知，或者使用标记的基准数据集。从这些模拟器、测试床和基准数据集到更加开放的领域的过渡给人工智能系统带来了重大挑战，包括领域复杂性的显着增加以及现实世界新奇事物的纳入；开放世界环境包含许多不属于人工智能系统训练集的分布外元素。在这里，我们提出了一种通用的、独立于领域的领域复杂性级别度量的路径。我们区分领域复杂性的两个方面：内在的和外在的。内在域复杂性是指在没有人工智能代理在该域上执行任务的任何操作或交互的情况下本身存在的复杂性。这是域复杂性的一个独立于代理的方面。外在域复杂性取决于代理和任务。内在和外在元素的结合体现了该领域的整体复杂性。我们以独立于域的角度构建定义和影响域复杂性级别的组件。与领域无关的复杂性测量可以定量预测人工智能系统从一个测试平台或环境过渡到另一个测试平台或环境时、在开放世界任务中面对分布外数据时、以及在开放世界域中遇到的快速扩展的解决方案和搜索空间时所面临的困难。 提出一种跨领域的复杂度度量方法，以量化AI系统在开放世界中的挑战 Katarina Doctor,Christine Task,Eric Kildebeck,Mayank Kejriwal,Lawrence Holder,Russell Leong cs.AI

authors_text: Cheng Chi,Zhenjia Xu,Siyuan Feng,Eric Cousineau,Yilun Du,Benjamin Burchfiel,Russ Tedrake,Shuran Song
title: Diffusion Policy: Visuomotor Policy Learning via Action Diffusion
title_zh: 扩散政策：通过行动扩散进行视觉运动政策学习
comment: An extended journal version of the original RSS2023 paper
comments: An extended journal version of the original RSS2023 paper
abstract: This paper introduces Diffusion Policy, a new way of generating robot behavior by representing a robot's visuomotor policy as a conditional denoising diffusion process. We benchmark Diffusion Policy across 12 different tasks from 4 different robot manipulation benchmarks and find that it consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art robot learning methods with an average improvement of 46.9%. Diffusion Policy learns the gradient of the action-distribution score function and iteratively optimizes with respect to this gradient field during inference via a series of stochastic Langevin dynamics steps. We find that the diffusion formulation yields powerful advantages when used for robot policies, including gracefully handling multimodal action distributions, being suitable for high-dimensional action spaces, and exhibiting impressive training stability. To fully unlock the potential of diffusion models for visuomotor policy learning on physical robots, this paper presents a set of key technical contributions including the incorporation of receding horizon control, visual conditioning, and the time-series diffusion transformer. We hope this work will help motivate a new generation of policy learning techniques that are able to leverage the powerful generative modeling capabilities of diffusion models. Code, data, and training details is publicly available diffusion-policy.cs.columbia.edu
summary: This paper introduces Diffusion Policy, a new way of generating robot behavior by representing a robot's visuomotor policy as a conditional denoising diffusion process. We benchmark Diffusion Policy across 12 different tasks from 4 different robot manipulation benchmarks and find that it consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art robot learning methods with an average improvement of 46.9%. Diffusion Policy learns the gradient of the action-distribution score function and iteratively optimizes with respect to this gradient field during inference via a series of stochastic Langevin dynamics steps. We find that the diffusion formulation yields powerful advantages when used for robot policies, including gracefully handling multimodal action distributions, being suitable for high-dimensional action spaces, and exhibiting impressive training stability. To fully unlock the potential of diffusion models for visuomotor policy learning on physical robots, this paper presents a set of key technical contributions including the incorporation of receding horizon control, visual conditioning, and the time-series diffusion transformer. We hope this work will help motivate a new generation of policy learning techniques that are able to leverage the powerful generative modeling capabilities of diffusion models. Code, data, and training details is publicly available diffusion-policy.cs.columbia.edu
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了扩散策略，这是一种通过将机器人的视觉运动策略表示为条件去噪扩散过程来生成机器人行为的新方法。我们对来自 4 个不同机器人操作基准的 12 个不同任务的扩散策略进行了基准测试，发现它始终优于现有最先进的机器人学习方法，平均提高了 46.9%。扩散策略学习动作分布得分函数的梯度，并在推理过程中通过一系列随机朗之万动力学步骤对该梯度场进行迭代优化。我们发现扩散公式在用于机器人策略时具有强大的优势，包括优雅地处理多模态动作分布、适用于高维动作空间以及表现出令人印象深刻的训练稳定性。为了充分释放扩散模型在物理机器人视觉运动策略学习中的潜力，本文提出了一系列关键技术贡献，包括结合后退地平线控制、视觉调节和时间序列扩散变压器。我们希望这项工作将有助于激发新一代政策学习技术，这些技术能够利用扩散模型强大的生成建模功能。代码、数据和培训详细信息可公开获取diffusion-policy.cs.columbia.edu

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于扩散模型的机器人视觉运动策略学习方法Diffusion Policy，通过条件去噪扩散过程生成机器人行为。
AI.motivation: 现有机器人学习方法在处理多模态动作分布、高维动作空间和训练稳定性方面存在局限，而扩散模型在生成建模中表现出强大潜力。
AI.method: 将视觉运动策略表示为条件去噪扩散过程，通过学习动作分布分数函数梯度并采用随机朗之万动力学迭代优化，结合后退时域控制、视觉条件和时间序列扩散变换器等关键技术。
AI.result: 在4个机器人操作基准的12个任务中平均超越现有最优方法46.9%，展现出对多模态分布处理、高维动作空间适应及训练稳定性的显著优势。
AI.conclusion: Diffusion Policy验证了扩散模型在机器人策略学习中的有效性，为下一代结合生成模型能力的策略学习技术提供了新方向。

title: Domain Randomization for Robust, Affordable and Effective Closed-loop Control of Soft Robots
title_zh: 用于鲁棒、经济且有效的软机器人闭环控制的域随机化
comment: Presented as conference paper at IEEE/RSJ IROS 2023, Detroit, USA. Project website at https://andreaprotopapa.github.io/dr-soro/
comments: Presented as conference paper at IEEE/RSJ IROS 2023, Detroit, USA. Project website at https://andreaprotopapa.github.io/dr-soro/
abstract: Soft robots are gaining popularity thanks to their intrinsic safety to contacts and adaptability. However, the potentially infinite number of Degrees of Freedom makes their modeling a daunting task, and in many cases only an approximated description is available. This challenge makes reinforcement learning (RL) based approaches inefficient when deployed on a realistic scenario, due to the large domain gap between models and the real platform. In this work, we demonstrate, for the first time, how Domain Randomization (DR) can solve this problem by enhancing RL policies for soft robots with: i) robustness w.r.t. unknown dynamics parameters; ii) reduced training times by exploiting drastically simpler dynamic models for learning; iii) better environment exploration, which can lead to exploitation of environmental constraints for optimal performance. Moreover, we introduce a novel algorithmic extension to previous adaptive domain randomization methods for the automatic inference of dynamics parameters for deformable objects. We provide an extensive evaluation in simulation on four different tasks and two soft robot designs, opening interesting perspectives for future research on Reinforcement Learning for closed-loop soft robot control.
summary: Soft robots are gaining popularity thanks to their intrinsic safety to contacts and adaptability. However, the potentially infinite number of Degrees of Freedom makes their modeling a daunting task, and in many cases only an approximated description is available. This challenge makes reinforcement learning (RL) based approaches inefficient when deployed on a realistic scenario, due to the large domain gap between models and the real platform. In this work, we demonstrate, for the first time, how Domain Randomization (DR) can solve this problem by enhancing RL policies for soft robots with: i) robustness w.r.t. unknown dynamics parameters; ii) reduced training times by exploiting drastically simpler dynamic models for learning; iii) better environment exploration, which can lead to exploitation of environmental constraints for optimal performance. Moreover, we introduce a novel algorithmic extension to previous adaptive domain randomization methods for the automatic inference of dynamics parameters for deformable objects. We provide an extensive evaluation in simulation on four different tasks and two soft robot designs, opening interesting perspectives for future research on Reinforcement Learning for closed-loop soft robot control.
abstract_zh: 软机器人因其接触安全性和适应性而越来越受欢迎。然而，潜在的无限数量的自由度使他们的建模成为一项艰巨的任务，并且在许多情况下只能提供近似的描述。由于模型和真实平台之间存在巨大的领域差距，这一挑战使得基于强化学习 (RL) 的方法在实际场景中部署时效率低下。在这项工作中，我们首次展示了域随机化 (DR) 如何通过增强软机器人的 RL 策略来解决这个问题：i) 鲁棒性。未知的动力学参数； ii) 通过利用极其简单的动态学习模型来减少训练时间； iii) 更好的环境探索，这可以导致利用环境约束来实现最佳性能。此外，我们对先前的自适应域随机化方法引入了一种新颖的算法扩展，用于自动推断可变形物体的动力学参数。我们对四种不同任务和两种软机器人设计进行了广泛的仿真评估，为闭环软机器人控制的强化学习的未来研究开辟了有趣的视角。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用领域随机化（DR）解决软机器人闭环控制中因模型与真实环境差距导致的强化学习（RL）效率低下问题，通过增强策略鲁棒性、减少训练时间并优化环境探索。
AI.motivation: 软机器人因自由度高导致建模困难，传统RL方法因模型与真实平台间存在显著领域差距而难以有效部署，需寻找提升鲁棒性和训练效率的解决方案。
AI.method: 提出结合领域随机化的RL策略优化方法，包括：1）通过随机化动力学参数提升鲁棒性；2）利用简化模型缩短训练时间；3）改进环境探索以利用约束条件。并扩展自适应DR算法以自动推断变形物体动力学参数。
AI.result: 在模拟环境中对四种任务和两种软机器人设计进行验证，证明方法能有效缩小领域差距，提升控制性能，为RL在软机器人闭环控制中的应用提供新思路。
AI.conclusion: 领域随机化可显著增强软机器人RL控制的鲁棒性与效率，自动推断动力学参数的算法扩展为未来研究提供了可行方向。

ai_content_json.result: 在模拟环境中对四种任务和两种软机器人设计进行验证，证明方法能有效缩小领域差距，提升控制性能，为RL在软机器人闭环控制中的应用提供新思路。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 领域随机化可显著增强软机器人RL控制的鲁棒性与效率，自动推断动力学参数的算法扩展为未来研究提供了可行方向。

tldr: 本文提出利用领域随机化（DR）解决软机器人闭环控制中因模型与真实环境差距导致的强化学习（RL）效率低下问题，通过增强策略鲁棒性、减少训练时间并优化环境探索。
motivation: 软机器人因自由度高导致建模困难，传统RL方法因模型与真实平台间存在显著领域差距而难以有效部署，需寻找提升鲁棒性和训练效率的解决方案。
method: 提出结合领域随机化的RL策略优化方法，包括：1）通过随机化动力学参数提升鲁棒性；2）利用简化模型缩短训练时间；3）改进环境探索以利用约束条件。并扩展自适应DR算法以自动推断变形物体动力学参数。
result: 在模拟环境中对四种任务和两种软机器人设计进行验证，证明方法能有效缩小领域差距，提升控制性能，为RL在软机器人闭环控制中的应用提供新思路。
conclusion: 领域随机化可显著增强软机器人RL控制的鲁棒性与效率，自动推断动力学参数的算法扩展为未来研究提供了可行方向。
search_text: Domain Randomization for Robust, Affordable and Effective Closed-loop Control of Soft Robots 用于鲁棒、经济且有效的软机器人闭环控制的域随机化 Soft robots are gaining popularity thanks to their intrinsic safety to contacts and adaptability. However, the potentially infinite number of Degrees of Freedom makes their modeling a daunting task, and in many cases only an approximated description is available. This challenge makes reinforcement learning (RL) based approaches inefficient when deployed on a realistic scenario, due to the large domain gap between models and the real platform. In this work, we demonstrate, for the first time, how Domain Randomization (DR) can solve this problem by enhancing RL policies for soft robots with: i) robustness w.r.t. unknown dynamics parameters; ii) reduced training times by exploiting drastically simpler dynamic models for learning; iii) better environment exploration, which can lead to exploitation of environmental constraints for optimal performance. Moreover, we introduce a novel algorithmic extension to previous adaptive domain randomization methods for the automatic inference of dynamics parameters for deformable objects. We provide an extensive evaluation in simulation on four different tasks and two soft robot designs, opening interesting perspectives for future research on Reinforcement Learning for closed-loop soft robot control. 软机器人因其接触安全性和适应性而越来越受欢迎。然而，潜在的无限数量的自由度使他们的建模成为一项艰巨的任务，并且在许多情况下只能提供近似的描述。由于模型和真实平台之间存在巨大的领域差距，这一挑战使得基于强化学习 (RL) 的方法在实际场景中部署时效率低下。在这项工作中，我们首次展示了域随机化 (DR) 如何通过增强软机器人的 RL 策略来解决这个问题：i) 鲁棒性。未知的动力学参数； ii) 通过利用极其简单的动态学习模型来减少训练时间； iii) 更好的环境探索，这可以导致利用环境约束来实现最佳性能。此外，我们对先前的自适应域随机化方法引入了一种新颖的算法扩展，用于自动推断可变形物体的动力学参数。我们对四种不同任务和两种软机器人设计进行了广泛的仿真评估，为闭环软机器人控制的强化学习的未来研究开辟了有趣的视角。 本文提出利用领域随机化（DR）解决软机器人闭环控制中因模型与真实环境差距导致的强化学习（RL）效率低下问题，通过增强策略鲁棒性、减少训练时间并优化环境探索。 Gabriele Tiboni,Andrea Protopapa,Tatiana Tommasi,Giuseppe Averta cs.RO,cs.LG

title: A Hybrid Architecture for Out of Domain Intent Detection and Intent Discovery
title_zh: 用于域外意图检测和意图发现的混合架构
abstract: Intent Detection is one of the tasks of the Natural Language Understanding (NLU) unit in task-oriented dialogue systems. Out of Scope (OOS) and Out of Domain (OOD) inputs may run these systems into a problem. On the other side, a labeled dataset is needed to train a model for Intent Detection in task-oriented dialogue systems. The creation of a labeled dataset is time-consuming and needs human resources. The purpose of this article is to address mentioned problems. The task of identifying OOD/OOS inputs is named OOD/OOS Intent Detection. Also, discovering new intents and pseudo-labeling of OOD inputs is well known by Intent Discovery. In OOD intent detection part, we make use of a Variational Autoencoder to distinguish between known and unknown intents independent of input data distribution. After that, an unsupervised clustering method is used to discover different unknown intents underlying OOD/OOS inputs. We also apply a non-linear dimensionality reduction on OOD/OOS representations to make distances between representations more meaning full for clustering. Our results show that the proposed model for both OOD/OOS Intent Detection and Intent Discovery achieves great results and passes baselines in English and Persian languages.
summary: Intent Detection is one of the tasks of the Natural Language Understanding (NLU) unit in task-oriented dialogue systems. Out of Scope (OOS) and Out of Domain (OOD) inputs may run these systems into a problem. On the other side, a labeled dataset is needed to train a model for Intent Detection in task-oriented dialogue systems. The creation of a labeled dataset is time-consuming and needs human resources. The purpose of this article is to address mentioned problems. The task of identifying OOD/OOS inputs is named OOD/OOS Intent Detection. Also, discovering new intents and pseudo-labeling of OOD inputs is well known by Intent Discovery. In OOD intent detection part, we make use of a Variational Autoencoder to distinguish between known and unknown intents independent of input data distribution. After that, an unsupervised clustering method is used to discover different unknown intents underlying OOD/OOS inputs. We also apply a non-linear dimensionality reduction on OOD/OOS representations to make distances between representations more meaning full for clustering. Our results show that the proposed model for both OOD/OOS Intent Detection and Intent Discovery achieves great results and passes baselines in English and Persian languages.
abstract_zh: 意图检测是面向任务的对话系统中自然语言理解（NLU）单元的任务之一。超出范围 (OOS) 和超出域 (OOD) 输入可能会让这些系统出现问题。另一方面，需要一个标记数据集来训练面向任务的对话系统中的意图检测模型。创建标记数据集非常耗时并且需要人力资源。本文的目的是解决上述问题。识别 OOD/OOS 输入的任务称为 OOD/OOS 意图检测。此外，意图发现还众所周知地发现 OOD 输入的新意图和伪标签。在 OOD 意图检测部分，我们利用变分自动编码器来区分已知和未知意图，而与输入数据分布无关。之后，使用无监督聚类方法来发现 OOD/OOS 输入背后的不同未知意图。我们还在 OOD/OOS 表示上应用非线性降维，以使表示之间的距离对于聚类来说更有意义。我们的结果表明，所提出的 OOD/OOS 意图检测和意图发现模型都取得了很好的结果，并通过了英语和波斯语的基线。

AI.tldr: 提出一种混合架构，解决任务导向对话系统中领域外意图检测与意图发现的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有系统难以处理领域外（OOD）和范围外（OOS）输入，且标注数据集的创建耗时耗力，需减少对人工标注的依赖。
AI.method: 结合变分自编码器（VAE）区分已知/未知意图，通过无监督聚类发现新意图，并采用非线性降维优化聚类效果。
AI.result: 模型在英文和波斯语数据集上均取得优于基线的结果，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该混合架构有效解决了OOD意图检测与意图发现的挑战，具有实际应用价值。

title: Exploiting Asymmetry for Synthetic Training Data Generation: SynthIE and the Case of Information Extraction
title_zh: 利用不对称性生成综合训练数据：SynthIE 和信息提取案例
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have great potential for synthetic data generation. This work shows that useful data can be synthetically generated even for tasks that cannot be solved directly by LLMs: for problems with structured outputs, it is possible to prompt an LLM to perform the task in the reverse direction, by generating plausible input text for a target output structure. Leveraging this asymmetry in task difficulty makes it possible to produce large-scale, high-quality data for complex tasks. We demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach on closed information extraction, where collecting ground-truth data is challenging, and no satisfactory dataset exists to date. We synthetically generate a dataset of 1.8M data points, establish its superior quality compared to existing datasets in a human evaluation, and use it to finetune small models (220M and 770M parameters), termed SynthIE, that outperform the prior state of the art (with equal model size) by a substantial margin of 57 absolute points in micro-F1 and 79 points in macro-F1. Code, data, and models are available at https://github.com/epfl-dlab/SynthIE.
summary: Large language models (LLMs) have great potential for synthetic data generation. This work shows that useful data can be synthetically generated even for tasks that cannot be solved directly by LLMs: for problems with structured outputs, it is possible to prompt an LLM to perform the task in the reverse direction, by generating plausible input text for a target output structure. Leveraging this asymmetry in task difficulty makes it possible to produce large-scale, high-quality data for complex tasks. We demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach on closed information extraction, where collecting ground-truth data is challenging, and no satisfactory dataset exists to date. We synthetically generate a dataset of 1.8M data points, establish its superior quality compared to existing datasets in a human evaluation, and use it to finetune small models (220M and 770M parameters), termed SynthIE, that outperform the prior state of the art (with equal model size) by a substantial margin of 57 absolute points in micro-F1 and 79 points in macro-F1. Code, data, and models are available at https://github.com/epfl-dlab/SynthIE.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 在合成数据生成方面具有巨大潜力。这项工作表明，即使对于法学硕士无法直接解决的任务，也可以综合生成有用的数据：对于结构化输出的问题，可以通过为目标输出结构生成合理的输入文本，提示法学硕士以相反的方向执行任务。利用任务难度的这种不对称性，可以为复杂任务生成大规模、高质量的数据。我们证明了这种方法在封闭信息提取方面的有效性，其中收集地面实况数据具有挑战性，并且迄今为止不存在令人满意的数据集。我们综合生成了 180 万个数据点的数据集，在人类评估中确立了其与现有数据集相比的卓越质量，并使用它来微调小型模型（220M 和 770M 参数），称为 SynthIE，其性能优于现有技术（模型大小相同），在微观 F1 中大幅领先 57 个绝对点，在宏观 F1 中领先 79 个绝对点。代码、数据和模型可在 https://github.com/epfl-dlab/SynthIE 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用大语言模型（LLM）在任务难度上的不对称性，通过逆向生成输入文本的方式，为信息抽取任务合成高质量训练数据。
AI.motivation: 封闭域信息抽取任务缺乏高质量标注数据，现有数据集难以满足需求，需探索新的数据生成方法。
AI.method: 通过提示LLM反向生成符合目标输出结构的输入文本，构建1.8M规模的合成数据集SynthIE，并用于微调小型模型。
AI.result: 合成数据集在人工评估中优于现有数据集，微调后的模型在微F1和宏F1指标上分别超越先前最优结果57和79个百分点。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了复杂任务的数据稀缺问题，为合成训练数据生成提供了新思路，显著提升了信息抽取模型性能。

authors_text: Sherry Yang,Ofir Nachum,Yilun Du,Jason Wei,Pieter Abbeel,Dale Schuurmans
title: Foundation Models for Decision Making: Problems, Methods, and Opportunities
title_zh: 决策的基础模型：问题、方法和机遇
abstract: Foundation models pretrained on diverse data at scale have demonstrated extraordinary capabilities in a wide range of vision and language tasks. When such models are deployed in real world environments, they inevitably interface with other entities and agents. For example, language models are often used to interact with human beings through dialogue, and visual perception models are used to autonomously navigate neighborhood streets. In response to these developments, new paradigms are emerging for training foundation models to interact with other agents and perform long-term reasoning. These paradigms leverage the existence of ever-larger datasets curated for multimodal, multitask, and generalist interaction. Research at the intersection of foundation models and decision making holds tremendous promise for creating powerful new systems that can interact effectively across a diverse range of applications such as dialogue, autonomous driving, healthcare, education, and robotics. In this manuscript, we examine the scope of foundation models for decision making, and provide conceptual tools and technical background for understanding the problem space and exploring new research directions. We review recent approaches that ground foundation models in practical decision making applications through a variety of methods such as prompting, conditional generative modeling, planning, optimal control, and reinforcement learning, and discuss common challenges and open problems in the field.

abstract_zh: 在各种数据上进行大规模预训练的基础模型在广泛的视觉和语言任务中表现出了非凡的能力。当此类模型部署在现实世界环境中时，它们不可避免地会与其他实体和代理进行交互。例如，语言模型通常用于通过对话与人类互动，视觉感知模型用于自动导航邻里街道。为了应对这些发展，新的范式正在出现，用于训练基础模型与其他智能体交互并执行长期推理。这些范例利用了为多模式、多任务和通才交互而策划的越来越大的数据集。基础模型和决策交叉点的研究为创建强大的新系统带来了巨大的希望，这些系统可以在对话、自动驾驶、医疗保健、教育和机器人等各种应用中有效地交互。在这篇手稿中，我们研究了决策基础模型的范围，并为理解问题空间和探索新的研究方向提供了概念工具和技术背景。我们回顾了通过提示、条件生成建模、规划、最优控制和强化学习等各种方法在实际决策应用中建立基础模型的最新方法，并讨论了该领域的常见挑战和开放问题。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了基础模型在决策任务中的应用，分析了其面临的挑战并总结了相关方法。
AI.motivation: 随着基础模型在现实环境中的广泛应用，如何使其有效与外部实体交互并进行长期推理成为关键问题。
AI.method: 通过回顾提示、条件生成建模、规划、最优控制及强化学习等方法，结合多模态和多任务数据集进行研究。
AI.result: 提供了决策任务中的概念工具和技术背景，梳理了当前方法与开放性问题。
AI.conclusion: 基础模型与决策结合具有巨大潜力，未来需在跨领域应用中解决交互性与长期推理等核心挑战。

authors_text: Nick Bührer,Zhejun Zhang,Alexander Liniger,Fisher Yu,Luc Van Gool
title: A Multiplicative Value Function for Safe and Efficient Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 用于安全高效强化学习的乘法值函数
abstract: An emerging field of sequential decision problems is safe Reinforcement Learning (RL), where the objective is to maximize the reward while obeying safety constraints. Being able to handle constraints is essential for deploying RL agents in real-world environments, where constraint violations can harm the agent and the environment. To this end, we propose a safe model-free RL algorithm with a novel multiplicative value function consisting of a safety critic and a reward critic. The safety critic predicts the probability of constraint violation and discounts the reward critic that only estimates constraint-free returns. By splitting responsibilities, we facilitate the learning task leading to increased sample efficiency. We integrate our approach into two popular RL algorithms, Proximal Policy Optimization and Soft Actor-Critic, and evaluate our method in four safety-focused environments, including classical RL benchmarks augmented with safety constraints and robot navigation tasks with images and raw Lidar scans as observations. Finally, we make the zero-shot sim-to-real transfer where a differential drive robot has to navigate through a cluttered room. Our code can be found at https://github.com/nikeke19/Safe-Mult-RL.
summary: An emerging field of sequential decision problems is safe Reinforcement Learning (RL), where the objective is to maximize the reward while obeying safety constraints. Being able to handle constraints is essential for deploying RL agents in real-world environments, where constraint violations can harm the agent and the environment. To this end, we propose a safe model-free RL algorithm with a novel multiplicative value function consisting of a safety critic and a reward critic. The safety critic predicts the probability of constraint violation and discounts the reward critic that only estimates constraint-free returns. By splitting responsibilities, we facilitate the learning task leading to increased sample efficiency. We integrate our approach into two popular RL algorithms, Proximal Policy Optimization and Soft Actor-Critic, and evaluate our method in four safety-focused environments, including classical RL benchmarks augmented with safety constraints and robot navigation tasks with images and raw Lidar scans as observations. Finally, we make the zero-shot sim-to-real transfer where a differential drive robot has to navigate through a cluttered room. Our code can be found at https://github.com/nikeke19/Safe-Mult-RL.
abstract_zh: 顺序决策问题的一个新兴领域是安全强化学习（RL），其目标是在遵守安全约束的同时最大化奖励。能够处理约束对于在现实环境中部署 RL 代理至关重要，在现实环境中，违反约束可能会损害代理和环境。为此，我们提出了一种安全的无模型强化学习算法，该算法具有由安全批评家和奖励批评家组成的新颖乘法值函数。安全批评家预测违反约束的概率，并低估仅估计无约束回报的奖励批评家。通过划分职责，我们可以促进学习任务，从而提高样本效率。我们将我们的方法集成到两种流行的 RL 算法中：Proximal Policy Optimization 和 Soft Actor-Critic，并在四种以安全为中心的环境中评估我们的方法，包括增强安全约束的经典 RL 基准以及以图像和原始激光雷达扫描作为观察的机器人导航任务。最后，我们进行零样本模拟到真实的转换，其中差动驱动机器人必须在杂乱的房间中导航。我们的代码可以在 https://github.com/nikeke19/Safe-Mult-RL 找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于乘法价值函数的安全高效强化学习算法，通过分离安全批评者和奖励批评者的职责，提升样本效率并确保约束遵守。
AI.motivation: 现实环境中约束违规可能对智能体和环境造成危害，传统方法难以平衡安全与效率，需设计兼顾安全性和样本效率的强化学习框架。
AI.method: 设计包含安全批评者（预测约束违规概率）和奖励批评者（估计无约束回报）的乘法价值函数，通过折扣机制整合二者，集成至PPO和Soft Actor-Critic算法中。
AI.result: 在四个安全导向环境中验证方法有效性，包括经典基准测试、机器人导航任务及零样本仿真到现实的迁移任务，均表现优于基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过职责分离提升学习效率，有效保障安全约束，为现实场景中的强化学习部署提供可行方案。

authors_text: Muhammad Aurangzeb Ahmad,Vijay Chickarmane,Farinaz Sabz Ali Pour,Nima Shariari,Taposh Dutta Roy
title: Validation of a Hospital Digital Twin with Machine Learning
title_zh: 使用机器学习验证医院数字孪生
abstract: Recently there has been a surge of interest in developing Digital Twins of process flows in healthcare to better understand bottlenecks and areas of improvement. A key challenge is in the validation process. We describe a work in progress for a digital twin using an agent based simulation model for determining bed turnaround time for patients in hospitals. We employ a strategy using machine learning for validating the model and implementing sensitivity analysis.
summary: Recently there has been a surge of interest in developing Digital Twins of process flows in healthcare to better understand bottlenecks and areas of improvement. A key challenge is in the validation process. We describe a work in progress for a digital twin using an agent based simulation model for determining bed turnaround time for patients in hospitals. We employ a strategy using machine learning for validating the model and implementing sensitivity analysis.
abstract_zh: 最近，人们对开发医疗保健流程数字孪生的兴趣激增，以更好地了解瓶颈和改进领域。一个关键的挑战在于验证过程。我们描述了一项正在进行的数字孪生工作，该工作使用基于代理的模拟模型来确定医院患者的床位周转时间。我们采用机器学习策略来验证模型并实施敏感性分析。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于机器学习的医院数字孪生验证方法，用于分析住院患者床位周转时间。
AI.motivation: 医疗流程中的数字孪生技术面临验证难题，亟需有效方法确保模型准确性以优化医院运营。
AI.method: 构建基于智能体的仿真模型模拟床位周转流程，并结合机器学习进行模型验证与敏感性分析。
AI.result: 初步验证了机器学习在数字孪生模型校准中的可行性，但需进一步完善数据与算法细节。
AI.conclusion: 机器学习为数字孪生验证提供了新路径，未来需结合更多临床数据提升模型可靠性。

ai_content_json.method: 构建基于智能体的仿真模型模拟床位周转流程，并结合机器学习进行模型验证与敏感性分析。
ai_content_json.result: 初步验证了机器学习在数字孪生模型校准中的可行性，但需进一步完善数据与算法细节。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 机器学习为数字孪生验证提供了新路径，未来需结合更多临床数据提升模型可靠性。

search_text: Validation of a Hospital Digital Twin with Machine Learning 使用机器学习验证医院数字孪生 Recently there has been a surge of interest in developing Digital Twins of process flows in healthcare to better understand bottlenecks and areas of improvement. A key challenge is in the validation process. We describe a work in progress for a digital twin using an agent based simulation model for determining bed turnaround time for patients in hospitals. We employ a strategy using machine learning for validating the model and implementing sensitivity analysis. 最近，人们对开发医疗保健流程数字孪生的兴趣激增，以更好地了解瓶颈和改进领域。一个关键的挑战在于验证过程。我们描述了一项正在进行的数字孪生工作，该工作使用基于代理的模拟模型来确定医院患者的床位周转时间。我们采用机器学习策略来验证模型并实施敏感性分析。 本文提出了一种基于机器学习的医院数字孪生验证方法，用于分析住院患者床位周转时间。 Muhammad Aurangzeb Ahmad,Vijay Chickarmane,Farinaz Sabz Ali Pour,Nima Shariari,Taposh Dutta Roy cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Zhejun Zhang,Alexander Liniger,Dengxin Dai,Fisher Yu,Luc Van Gool
title: TrafficBots: Towards World Models for Autonomous Driving Simulation and Motion Prediction
title_zh: TrafficBots：走向自动驾驶模拟和运动预测的世界模型
comment: Published at ICRA 2023. The repository is available at https://github.com/zhejz/TrafficBots
comments: Published at ICRA 2023. The repository is available at https://github.com/zhejz/TrafficBots
abstract: Data-driven simulation has become a favorable way to train and test autonomous driving algorithms. The idea of replacing the actual environment with a learned simulator has also been explored in model-based reinforcement learning in the context of world models. In this work, we show data-driven traffic simulation can be formulated as a world model. We present TrafficBots, a multi-agent policy built upon motion prediction and end-to-end driving, and based on TrafficBots we obtain a world model tailored for the planning module of autonomous vehicles. Existing data-driven traffic simulators are lacking configurability and scalability. To generate configurable behaviors, for each agent we introduce a destination as navigational information, and a time-invariant latent personality that specifies the behavioral style. To improve the scalability, we present a new scheme of positional encoding for angles, allowing all agents to share the same vectorized context and the use of an architecture based on dot-product attention. As a result, we can simulate all traffic participants seen in dense urban scenarios. Experiments on the Waymo open motion dataset show TrafficBots can simulate realistic multi-agent behaviors and achieve good performance on the motion prediction task.
summary: Data-driven simulation has become a favorable way to train and test autonomous driving algorithms. The idea of replacing the actual environment with a learned simulator has also been explored in model-based reinforcement learning in the context of world models. In this work, we show data-driven traffic simulation can be formulated as a world model. We present TrafficBots, a multi-agent policy built upon motion prediction and end-to-end driving, and based on TrafficBots we obtain a world model tailored for the planning module of autonomous vehicles. Existing data-driven traffic simulators are lacking configurability and scalability. To generate configurable behaviors, for each agent we introduce a destination as navigational information, and a time-invariant latent personality that specifies the behavioral style. To improve the scalability, we present a new scheme of positional encoding for angles, allowing all agents to share the same vectorized context and the use of an architecture based on dot-product attention. As a result, we can simulate all traffic participants seen in dense urban scenarios. Experiments on the Waymo open motion dataset show TrafficBots can simulate realistic multi-agent behaviors and achieve good performance on the motion prediction task.
abstract_zh: 数据驱动仿真已成为训练和测试自动驾驶算法的有利方式。在世界模型背景下的基于模型的强化学习中，也探索了用学习的模拟器替换实际环境的想法。在这项工作中，我们展示了数据驱动的交通模拟可以制定为世界模型。我们提出了 TrafficBots，一种基于运动预测和端到端驾驶的多智能体策略，基于 TrafficBots，我们获得了为自动驾驶车辆的规划模块量身定制的世界模型。现有的数据驱动的交通模拟器缺乏可配置性和可扩展性。为了生成可配置的行为，我们为每个代理引入一个目的地作为导航信息，以及指定行为风格的时不变的潜在个性。为了提高可扩展性，我们提出了一种新的角度位置编码方案，允许所有代理共享相同的向量化上下文并使用基于点积注意力的架构。因此，我们可以模拟密集城市场景中看到的所有交通参与者。 Waymo 开放运动数据集上的实验表明，TrafficBots 可以模拟真实的多智能体行为，并在运动预测任务上取得良好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出TrafficBots，一种基于世界模型的多智能体策略，用于自动驾驶的交通仿真和运动预测。
AI.motivation: 现有数据驱动交通模拟器缺乏可配置性和可扩展性，难以满足自动驾驶算法训练与测试需求。
AI.method: 为每个智能体引入目的地和时间不变的行为风格参数，并设计角度位置编码与点积注意力机制，实现共享上下文的可扩展仿真。
AI.result: 在Waymo数据集上验证了TrafficBots可模拟密集城市场景的多智能体真实行为，运动预测任务表现优异。
AI.conclusion: TrafficBots通过行为参数化和架构创新，解决了交通模拟的可配置性与可扩展性问题，为自动驾驶提供有效仿真工具。

The idea of replacing the actual environment with a learned simulator has also been explored in model-based reinforcement learning in the context of world models. As a result, we can simulate all traffic participants seen in dense urban scenarios. authors_text: Hong Yang,William Gebhardt,Alexander G. Ororbia,Travis Desell
title: Predicted Embedding Power Regression for Large-Scale Out-of-Distribution Detection
title_zh: 用于大规模分布外检测的预测嵌入功率回归
abstract: Out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs can compromise the performance and safety of real world machine learning systems. While many methods exist for OOD detection and work well on small scale datasets with lower resolution and few classes, few methods have been developed for large-scale OOD detection. Existing large-scale methods generally depend on maximum classification probability, such as the state-of-the-art grouped softmax method. In this work, we develop a novel approach that calculates the probability of the predicted class label based on label distributions learned during the training process. Our method performs better than current state-of-the-art methods with only a negligible increase in compute cost. We evaluate our method against contemporary methods across $14$ datasets and achieve a statistically significant improvement with respect to AUROC (84.2 vs 82.4) and AUPR (96.2 vs 93.7).
summary: Out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs can compromise the performance and safety of real world machine learning systems. While many methods exist for OOD detection and work well on small scale datasets with lower resolution and few classes, few methods have been developed for large-scale OOD detection. Existing large-scale methods generally depend on maximum classification probability, such as the state-of-the-art grouped softmax method. In this work, we develop a novel approach that calculates the probability of the predicted class label based on label distributions learned during the training process. Our method performs better than current state-of-the-art methods with only a negligible increase in compute cost. We evaluate our method against contemporary methods across $14$ datasets and achieve a statistically significant improvement with respect to AUROC (84.2 vs 82.4) and AUPR (96.2 vs 93.7).
abstract_zh: 分布外 (OOD) 输入可能会损害现实世界机器学习系统的性能和安全性。虽然存在许多用于 OOD 检测的方法，并且在分辨率较低和类别较少的小规模数据集上运行良好，但针对大规模 OOD 检测开发的方法很少。现有的大规模方法通常依赖于最大分类概率，例如最先进的分组 softmax 方法。在这项工作中，我们开发了一种新颖的方法，可以根据训练过程中学习的标签分布来计算预测类标签的概率。我们的方法比当前最先进的方法表现得更好，而计算成本的增加几乎可以忽略不计。我们在 14 美元的数据集上对照当代方法评估了我们的方法，并在 AUROC（84.2 vs 82.4）和 AUPR（96.2 vs 93.7）方面取得了统计上的显着改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种用于大规模分布外检测的新方法，通过预测嵌入功率回归提升检测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有分布外检测方法在小规模数据集上表现良好，但难以适应大规模场景，且依赖最大分类概率的方法存在局限性。
AI.method: 基于训练过程中学习到的标签分布，计算预测类别标签的概率，构建新的回归模型进行检测。
AI.result: 在14个数据集上实验显示，该方法在AUROC（84.2 vs 82.4）和AUPR（96.2 vs 93.7）指标上均显著优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 新方法在保持计算成本几乎不变的情况下，有效提升了大规模分布外检测的准确性。

authors_text: Yonatan Dukler,Alessandro Achille,Hao Yang,Varsha Vivek,Luca Zancato,Benjamin Bowman,Avinash Ravichandran,Charless Fowlkes,Ashwin Swaminathan,Stefano Soatto
title: Your representations are in the network: composable and parallel adaptation for large scale models
title_zh: 您的表示位于网络中：大型模型的可组合和并行适应
abstract: We propose InCA, a lightweight method for transfer learning that cross-attends to any activation layer of a pre-trained model. During training, InCA uses a single forward pass to extract multiple activations, which are passed to external cross-attention adapters, trained anew and combined or selected for downstream tasks. We show that, even when selecting a single top-scoring adapter, InCA achieves performance comparable to full fine-tuning, at a cost comparable to fine-tuning just the last layer. For example, with a cross-attention probe 1.3% the size of a pre-trained ViT-L/16 model, we achieve performance within 0.2% of the full fine-tuning paragon at a computational training cost of 51% of the baseline, on average across 11 downstream classification. Unlike other forms of efficient adaptation, InCA does not require backpropagating through the pre-trained model, thus leaving its execution unaltered at both training and inference. The versatility of InCA is best illustrated in fine-grained tasks, which may require accessing information absent in the last layer but accessible in intermediate layer activations. Since the backbone is fixed, InCA allows parallel ensembling as well as parallel execution of multiple tasks. InCA achieves state-of-the-art performance in the ImageNet-to-Sketch multi-task benchmark.
summary: We propose InCA, a lightweight method for transfer learning that cross-attends to any activation layer of a pre-trained model. During training, InCA uses a single forward pass to extract multiple activations, which are passed to external cross-attention adapters, trained anew and combined or selected for downstream tasks. We show that, even when selecting a single top-scoring adapter, InCA achieves performance comparable to full fine-tuning, at a cost comparable to fine-tuning just the last layer. For example, with a cross-attention probe 1.3% the size of a pre-trained ViT-L/16 model, we achieve performance within 0.2% of the full fine-tuning paragon at a computational training cost of 51% of the baseline, on average across 11 downstream classification. Unlike other forms of efficient adaptation, InCA does not require backpropagating through the pre-trained model, thus leaving its execution unaltered at both training and inference. The versatility of InCA is best illustrated in fine-grained tasks, which may require accessing information absent in the last layer but accessible in intermediate layer activations. Since the backbone is fixed, InCA allows parallel ensembling as well as parallel execution of multiple tasks. InCA achieves state-of-the-art performance in the ImageNet-to-Sketch multi-task benchmark.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 InCA，一种轻量级的迁移学习方法，可以交叉参与预训练模型的任何激活层。在训练期间，InCA 使用单个前向传递来提取多个激活，这些激活被传递给外部交叉注意适配器，重新训练并组合或选择用于下游任务。我们表明，即使选择单个得分最高的适配器，InCA 也能实现与完全微调相当的性能，而成本与仅微调最后一层相当。例如，使用预训练 ViT-L/16 模型大小 1.3% 的交叉注意力探针，我们在 11 个下游分类中的平均计算训练成本为基线的 51%，实现了完全微调典范的 0.2% 以内的性能。与其他形式的高效适应不同，InCA 不需要通过预训练模型进行反向传播，因此其执行在训练和推理时都保持不变。 InCA 的多功能性在细粒度任务中得到了最好的体现，这些任务可能需要访问最后一层中不存在但在中间层激活中可以访问的信息。由于主干是固定的，InCA 允许并行集成以及多个任务的并行执行。 InCA 在 ImageNet-to-Sketch 多任务基准测试中实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出InCA方法，通过跨注意力适配器实现大规模预训练模型的轻量级迁移学习，无需修改原模型参数。
AI.motivation: 传统微调方法计算成本高且需修改预训练模型，而现有高效适配方法难以兼顾性能与灵活性。本文旨在开发一种无需反向传播、支持并行任务的轻量级适配方案。
AI.method: InCA通过单次前向传播提取多层激活特征，将外部跨注意力适配器与预训练模型解耦，通过适配器组合或选择实现下游任务适配，保持主干模型参数冻结。
AI.result: 在11个分类任务中，单个适配器性能接近全量微调（误差<0.2%），训练成本仅为基线的51%；在ImageNet-to-Sketch多任务基准中达到SOTA，且支持并行集成。
AI.conclusion: InCA通过解耦适配器设计实现了高效、灵活的模型适配，在保持原模型完整性的前提下，为大规模模型的多任务学习提供了新范式。

search_text: Your representations are in the network: composable and parallel adaptation for large scale models 您的表示位于网络中：大型模型的可组合和并行适应 We propose InCA, a lightweight method for transfer learning that cross-attends to any activation layer of a pre-trained model. During training, InCA uses a single forward pass to extract multiple activations, which are passed to external cross-attention adapters, trained anew and combined or selected for downstream tasks. We show that, even when selecting a single top-scoring adapter, InCA achieves performance comparable to full fine-tuning, at a cost comparable to fine-tuning just the last layer. For example, with a cross-attention probe 1.3% the size of a pre-trained ViT-L/16 model, we achieve performance within 0.2% of the full fine-tuning paragon at a computational training cost of 51% of the baseline, on average across 11 downstream classification. Unlike other forms of efficient adaptation, InCA does not require backpropagating through the pre-trained model, thus leaving its execution unaltered at both training and inference. The versatility of InCA is best illustrated in fine-grained tasks, which may require accessing information absent in the last layer but accessible in intermediate layer activations. Since the backbone is fixed, InCA allows parallel ensembling as well as parallel execution of multiple tasks. InCA achieves state-of-the-art performance in the ImageNet-to-Sketch multi-task benchmark. 我们提出了 InCA，一种轻量级的迁移学习方法，可以交叉参与预训练模型的任何激活层。在训练期间，InCA 使用单个前向传递来提取多个激活，这些激活被传递给外部交叉注意适配器，重新训练并组合或选择用于下游任务。我们表明，即使选择单个得分最高的适配器，InCA 也能实现与完全微调相当的性能，而成本与仅微调最后一层相当。例如，使用预训练 ViT-L/16 模型大小 1.3% 的交叉注意力探针，我们在 11 个下游分类中的平均计算训练成本为基线的 51%，实现了完全微调典范的 0.2% 以内的性能。与其他形式的高效适应不同，InCA 不需要通过预训练模型进行反向传播，因此其执行在训练和推理时都保持不变。 InCA 的多功能性在细粒度任务中得到了最好的体现，这些任务可能需要访问最后一层中不存在但在中间层激活中可以访问的信息。由于主干是固定的，InCA 允许并行集成以及多个任务的并行执行。 InCA 在 ImageNet-to-Sketch 多任务基准测试中实现了最先进的性能。 本文提出InCA方法，通过跨注意力适配器实现大规模预训练模型的轻量级迁移学习，无需修改原模型参数。 Yonatan Dukler,Alessandro Achille,Hao Yang,Varsha Vivek,Luca Zancato,Benjamin Bowman,Avinash Ravichandran,Charless Fowlkes,Ashwin Swaminathan,Stefano Soatto cs.LG,cs.CV

authors_text: Dat Ngo,Lam Pham,Huy Phan,Minh Tran,Delaram Jarchi,Sefki Kolozali
title: An Inception-Residual-Based Architecture with Multi-Objective Loss for Detecting Respiratory Anomalies
title_zh: 用于检测呼吸异常的具有多目标损失的基于初始残差的架构
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning system applied for detecting anomalies from respiratory sound recordings. Initially, our system begins with audio feature extraction using Gammatone and Continuous Wavelet transformation. This step aims to transform the respiratory sound input into a two-dimensional spectrogram where both spectral and temporal features are presented. Then, our proposed system integrates Inception-residual-based backbone models combined with multi-head attention and multi-objective loss to classify respiratory anomalies. Instead of applying a simple concatenation approach by combining results from various spectrograms, we propose a Linear combination, which has the ability to regulate equally the contribution of each individual spectrogram throughout the training process. To evaluate the performance, we conducted experiments over the benchmark dataset of SPRSound (The Open-Source SJTU Paediatric Respiratory Sound) proposed by the IEEE BioCAS 2022 challenge. As regards the Score computed by an average between the average score and harmonic score, our proposed system gained significant improvements of 9.7%, 15.8%, 17.8%, and 16.1% in Task 1-1, Task 1-2, Task 2-1, and Task 2-2, respectively, compared to the challenge baseline system. Notably, we achieved the Top-1 performance in Task 2-1 and Task 2-2 with the highest Score of 74.5% and 53.9%, respectively.
summary: This paper presents a deep learning system applied for detecting anomalies from respiratory sound recordings. Initially, our system begins with audio feature extraction using Gammatone and Continuous Wavelet transformation. This step aims to transform the respiratory sound input into a two-dimensional spectrogram where both spectral and temporal features are presented. Then, our proposed system integrates Inception-residual-based backbone models combined with multi-head attention and multi-objective loss to classify respiratory anomalies. Instead of applying a simple concatenation approach by combining results from various spectrograms, we propose a Linear combination, which has the ability to regulate equally the contribution of each individual spectrogram throughout the training process. To evaluate the performance, we conducted experiments over the benchmark dataset of SPRSound (The Open-Source SJTU Paediatric Respiratory Sound) proposed by the IEEE BioCAS 2022 challenge. As regards the Score computed by an average between the average score and harmonic score, our proposed system gained significant improvements of 9.7%, 15.8%, 17.8%, and 16.1% in Task 1-1, Task 1-2, Task 2-1, and Task 2-2, respectively, compared to the challenge baseline system. Notably, we achieved the Top-1 performance in Task 2-1 and Task 2-2 with the highest Score of 74.5% and 53.9%, respectively.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种用于检测呼吸录音异常的深度学习系统。最初，我们的系统从使用 Gammatone 和连续小波变换提取音频特征开始。此步骤的目的是将呼吸音输入转换为二维频谱图，其中呈现频谱和时间特征。然后，我们提出的系统集成了基于 Inception-residual 的骨干模型，并结合多头注意力和多目标损失来对呼吸异常进行分类。我们提出了一种线性组合，而不是通过组合不同频谱图的结果来应用简单的串联方法，它能够在整个训练过程中平等地调节每个单独频谱图的贡献。为了评估性能，我们对 IEEE BioCAS 2022 挑战赛提出的 SPRSound（开源上海交通大学儿科呼吸音）基准数据集进行了实验。至于通过平均分数和调和分数之间的平均值计算的分数，与挑战基线系统相比，我们提出的系统在任务 1-1、任务 1-2、任务 2-1 和任务 2-2 中分别获得了 9.7%、15.8%、17.8% 和 16.1% 的显着改进。值得注意的是，我们在任务 2-1 和任务 2-2 中取得了 Top-1 的表现，最高分分别为 74.5% 和 53.9%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Inception-残差结构和多目标损失函数的深度学习系统，用于检测呼吸音异常。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理呼吸音的时频特征时存在局限性，需结合多尺度特征提取与多目标优化以提升异常检测性能。
AI.method: 采用Gammatone和连续小波变换生成二维时频谱图，融合Inception-残差网络与多头注意力机制，并通过线性组合策略平衡多谱图贡献，结合多目标损失函数进行训练。
AI.result: 在SPRSound数据集上，任务1-1至2-2的得分分别提升9.7%、15.8%、17.8%和16.1%，并在任务2-1和2-2中取得最高分74.5%和53.9%。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过多尺度特征融合与多目标优化有效提升了呼吸异常检测性能，验证了模型设计的优越性。

authors_text: Wei Xi,Yongxin Zhang,Changnan Xiao,Xuefeng Huang,Shihong Deng,Haowei Liang,Jie Chen,Peng Sun
title: Mastering Strategy Card Game (Legends of Code and Magic) via End-to-End Policy and Optimistic Smooth Fictitious Play
title_zh: 通过端到端策略和乐观流畅的虚拟游戏掌握策略卡牌游戏（《代码与魔法传奇》）
abstract: Deep Reinforcement Learning combined with Fictitious Play shows impressive results on many benchmark games, most of which are, however, single-stage. In contrast, real-world decision making problems may consist of multiple stages, where the observation spaces and the action spaces can be completely different across stages. We study a two-stage strategy card game Legends of Code and Magic and propose an end-to-end policy to address the difficulties that arise in multi-stage game. We also propose an optimistic smooth fictitious play algorithm to find the Nash Equilibrium for the two-player game. Our approach wins double championships of COG2022 competition. Extensive studies verify and show the advancement of our approach.
summary: Deep Reinforcement Learning combined with Fictitious Play shows impressive results on many benchmark games, most of which are, however, single-stage. In contrast, real-world decision making problems may consist of multiple stages, where the observation spaces and the action spaces can be completely different across stages. We study a two-stage strategy card game Legends of Code and Magic and propose an end-to-end policy to address the difficulties that arise in multi-stage game. We also propose an optimistic smooth fictitious play algorithm to find the Nash Equilibrium for the two-player game. Our approach wins double championships of COG2022 competition. Extensive studies verify and show the advancement of our approach.
abstract_zh: 深度强化学习与虚构游戏相结合，在许多基准游戏中显示出令人印象深刻的结果，但其中大多数都是单阶段游戏。相反，现实世界的决策问题可能由多个阶段组成，其中观察空间和行动空间在不同阶段可能完全不同。我们研究了两阶段策略卡牌游戏《Code and Magic》，并提出了一种端到端策略来解决多阶段游戏中出现的困难。我们还提出了一种乐观平滑的虚拟游戏算法来找到两人游戏的纳什均衡。我们的方法赢得了COG2022竞赛的双冠。广泛的研究验证并展示了我们方法的进步。

AI.tldr: 本文通过端到端策略与乐观平滑拟态博弈算法，在两阶段策略卡牌游戏《Legends of Code and Magic》中实现高效决策。
AI.motivation: 现有深度强化学习与拟态博弈方法多针对单阶段游戏，难以应对现实多阶段决策中观察空间与动作空间动态变化的挑战。
AI.method: 提出端到端策略应对多阶段游戏复杂性，并设计乐观平滑拟态博弈算法以求解两人零和博弈的纳什均衡。
AI.result: 该方法在COG2022竞赛中斩获双冠，实验验证其在多阶段决策任务中的优越性。
AI.conclusion: 端到端策略与优化拟态博弈的结合有效解决了多阶段游戏的决策难题，为复杂博弈场景提供新思路。

search_text: Mastering Strategy Card Game (Legends of Code and Magic) via End-to-End Policy and Optimistic Smooth Fictitious Play 通过端到端策略和乐观流畅的虚拟游戏掌握策略卡牌游戏（《代码与魔法传奇》） Deep Reinforcement Learning combined with Fictitious Play shows impressive results on many benchmark games, most of which are, however, single-stage. In contrast, real-world decision making problems may consist of multiple stages, where the observation spaces and the action spaces can be completely different across stages. We study a two-stage strategy card game Legends of Code and Magic and propose an end-to-end policy to address the difficulties that arise in multi-stage game. We also propose an optimistic smooth fictitious play algorithm to find the Nash Equilibrium for the two-player game. Our approach wins double championships of COG2022 competition. Extensive studies verify and show the advancement of our approach. 深度强化学习与虚构游戏相结合，在许多基准游戏中显示出令人印象深刻的结果，但其中大多数都是单阶段游戏。相反，现实世界的决策问题可能由多个阶段组成，其中观察空间和行动空间在不同阶段可能完全不同。我们研究了两阶段策略卡牌游戏《Code and Magic》，并提出了一种端到端策略来解决多阶段游戏中出现的困难。我们还提出了一种乐观平滑的虚拟游戏算法来找到两人游戏的纳什均衡。我们的方法赢得了COG2022竞赛的双冠。广泛的研究验证并展示了我们方法的进步。 本文通过端到端策略与乐观平滑拟态博弈算法，在两阶段策略卡牌游戏《Legends of Code and Magic》中实现高效决策。 Wei Xi,Yongxin Zhang,Changnan Xiao,Xuefeng Huang,Shihong Deng,Haowei Liang,Jie Chen,Peng Sun cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.GT

authors_text: Giacomo Camposampiero,Loic Houmard,Benjamin Estermann,Joël Mathys,Roger Wattenhofer
title: Abstract Visual Reasoning Enabled by Language
title_zh: 语言支持的抽象视觉推理
comment: The first two authors have contributed equally to this work. Accepted as regular paper at CVPR 2023 Workshop and Challenges for New Frontiers in Visual Language Reasoning: Compositionality, Prompts and Causality (NFVLR)
comments: The first two authors have contributed equally to this work. Accepted as regular paper at CVPR 2023 Workshop and Challenges for New Frontiers in Visual Language Reasoning: Compositionality, Prompts and Causality (NFVLR)
abstract: While artificial intelligence (AI) models have achieved human or even superhuman performance in many well-defined applications, they still struggle to show signs of broad and flexible intelligence. The Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC), a visual intelligence benchmark introduced by François Chollet, aims to assess how close AI systems are to human-like cognitive abilities. Most current approaches rely on carefully handcrafted domain-specific program searches to brute-force solutions for the tasks present in ARC. In this work, we propose a general learning-based framework for solving ARC. It is centered on transforming tasks from the vision to the language domain. This composition of language and vision allows for pre-trained models to be leveraged at each stage, enabling a shift from handcrafted priors towards the learned priors of the models. While not yet beating state-of-the-art models on ARC, we demonstrate the potential of our approach, for instance, by solving some ARC tasks that have not been solved previously.
summary: While artificial intelligence (AI) models have achieved human or even superhuman performance in many well-defined applications, they still struggle to show signs of broad and flexible intelligence. The Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC), a visual intelligence benchmark introduced by François Chollet, aims to assess how close AI systems are to human-like cognitive abilities. Most current approaches rely on carefully handcrafted domain-specific program searches to brute-force solutions for the tasks present in ARC. In this work, we propose a general learning-based framework for solving ARC. It is centered on transforming tasks from the vision to the language domain. This composition of language and vision allows for pre-trained models to be leveraged at each stage, enabling a shift from handcrafted priors towards the learned priors of the models. While not yet beating state-of-the-art models on ARC, we demonstrate the potential of our approach, for instance, by solving some ARC tasks that have not been solved previously.
abstract_zh: 尽管人工智能 (AI) 模型在许多定义明确的应用中已经实现了人类甚至超人类的性能，但它们仍然难以表现出广泛而灵活的智能。抽象与推理语料库（ARC）是 François Chollet 提出的视觉智能基准，旨在评估人工智能系统与人类认知能力的接近程度。当前大多数方法依赖于精心设计的特定领域程序搜索，为 ARC 中存在的任务提供强力解决方案。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个用于解决 ARC 的通用的基于学习的框架。它的重点是将任务从视觉领域转换到语言领域。语言和视觉的这种组合允许在每个阶段利用预先训练的模型，从而实现从手工设计的先验到学习的模型先验的转变。虽然尚未击败 ARC 上最先进的模型，但我们展示了我们的方法的潜力，例如，通过解决一些以前尚未解决的 ARC 任务。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于学习的通用框架，通过将视觉任务转化为语言领域来解决ARC基准测试中的抽象视觉推理问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖手工设计的领域特定程序搜索，缺乏灵活性和泛化能力，难以应对ARC基准测试中需要广泛认知能力的挑战。
AI.method: 构建语言与视觉结合的框架，利用预训练模型分别处理视觉和语言模态，通过跨模态转换实现任务求解。
AI.result: 未超越当前最优模型，但成功解决了部分此前未被解决的ARC任务，验证了方法的可行性与潜力。
AI.conclusion: 该框架展示了语言驱动的视觉推理新思路，未来可通过优化跨模态对齐和模型规模进一步提升性能。

authors_text: Haimin Luo,Siyuan Zhang,Fuqiang Zhao,Haotian Jing,Penghao Wang,Zhenxiao Yu,Dongxue Yan,Junran Ding,Boyuan Zhang,Qiang Hu,Shu Yin,Lan Xu,JIngyi Yu
title: NEPHELE: A Neural Platform for Highly Realistic Cloud Radiance Rendering
title_zh: NEPHELE：用于高度逼真的云辐射渲染的神经平台
abstract: We have recently seen tremendous progress in neural rendering (NR) advances, i.e., NeRF, for photo-real free-view synthesis. Yet, as a local technique based on a single computer/GPU, even the best-engineered Instant-NGP or i-NGP cannot reach real-time performance when rendering at a high resolution, and often requires huge local computing resources. In this paper, we resort to cloud rendering and present NEPHELE, a neural platform for highly realistic cloud radiance rendering. In stark contrast with existing NR approaches, our NEPHELE allows for more powerful rendering capabilities by combining multiple remote GPUs and facilitates collaboration by allowing multiple people to view the same NeRF scene simultaneously. We introduce i-NOLF to employ opacity light fields for ultra-fast neural radiance rendering in a one-query-per-ray manner. We further resemble the Lumigraph with geometry proxies for fast ray querying and subsequently employ a small MLP to model the local opacity lumishperes for high-quality rendering. We also adopt Perfect Spatial Hashing in i-NOLF to enhance cache coherence. As a result, our i-NOLF achieves an order of magnitude performance gain in terms of efficiency than i-NGP, especially for the multi-user multi-viewpoint setting under cloud rendering scenarios. We further tailor a task scheduler accompanied by our i-NOLF representation and demonstrate the advance of our methodological design through a comprehensive cloud platform, consisting of a series of cooperated modules, i.e., render farms, task assigner, frame composer, and detailed streaming strategies. Using such a cloud platform compatible with neural rendering, we further showcase the capabilities of our cloud radiance rendering through a series of applications, ranging from cloud VR/AR rendering.

Yet, as a local technique based on a single computer/GPU, even the best-engineered Instant-NGP or i-NGP cannot reach real-time performance when rendering at a high resolution, and often requires huge local computing resources. In this paper, we resort to cloud rendering and present NEPHELE, a neural platform for highly realistic cloud radiance rendering. In stark contrast with existing NR approaches, our NEPHELE allows for more powerful rendering capabilities by combining multiple remote GPUs and facilitates collaboration by allowing multiple people to view the same NeRF scene simultaneously. We also adopt Perfect Spatial Hashing in i-NOLF to enhance cache coherence. As a result, our i-NOLF achieves an order of magnitude performance gain in terms of efficiency than i-NGP, especially for the multi-user multi-viewpoint setting under cloud rendering scenarios. We further tailor a task scheduler accompanied by our i-NOLF representation and demonstrate the advance of our methodological design through a comprehensive cloud platform, consisting of a series of cooperated modules, i.e., render farms, task assigner, frame composer, and detailed streaming strategies. Using such a cloud platform compatible with neural rendering, we further showcase the capabilities of our cloud radiance rendering through a series of applications, ranging from cloud VR/AR rendering.
abstract_zh: 我们最近看到了神经渲染 (NR) 方面的巨大进步，即用于照片真实自由视图合成的 NeRF。然而，作为一种基于单台计算机/GPU的本地技术，即使是设计最好的Instant-NGP或i-NGP在高分辨率渲染时也无法达到实时性能，并且往往需要巨大的本地计算资源。在本文中，我们采用云渲染并提出了 NEPHELE，一个用于高度逼真的云辐射渲染的神经平台。与现有的 NR 方法形成鲜明对比，我们的 NEPHELE 通过组合多个远程 GPU 实现更强大的渲染功能，并通过允许多人同时查看同一 NeRF 场景来促进协作。我们引入 i-NOLF，以每条光线一次查询的方式采用不透明光场进行超快速神经辐射渲染。我们进一步类似于具有几何代理的 Lumigraph，用于快速光线查询，随后采用小型 MLP 对局部不透明度 lumishperes 进行建模，以实现高质量渲染。我们还在 i-NOLF 中采用 Perfect Spatial Hashing 来增强缓存一致性。因此，我们的i-NOLF在效率方面比i-NGP实现了一个数量级的性能增益，特别是对于云渲染场景下的多用户多视点设置。我们进一步定制了一个带有 i-NOLF 表示的任务调度器，并通过一个综合的云平台展示了我们方法设计的先进性，该平台由一系列协作模块组成，即渲染农场、任务分配器、帧编辑器和详细的流策略。利用这样一个兼容神经渲染的云平台，我们通过云VR/AR渲染等一系列应用进一步展示了我们的云辐射渲染的能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出NEPHELE，一个基于云端的神经渲染平台，通过结合多GPU资源和优化算法实现高质量实时云辐射度渲染。
AI.motivation: 现有神经渲染技术（如NeRF）在高分辨率下难以实现实时性能且依赖本地强大算力，无法满足多用户协作需求。
AI.method: 设计i-NOLF算法结合不透明度光场与几何代理加速渲染，采用Perfect Spatial Hashing优化缓存，并构建包含任务调度器、渲染农场等模块的云端协作平台。
AI.result: i-NOLF效率较i-NGP提升一个数量级，尤其在多用户多视角场景下表现优异，成功应用于云VR/AR等场景。
AI.conclusion: NEPHELE通过云端分布式计算与算法优化，显著提升了神经渲染的实时性与扩展性，为多用户协作场景提供了新解决方案。

result: i-NOLF效率较i-NGP提升一个数量级，尤其在多用户多视角场景下表现优异，成功应用于云VR/AR等场景。
conclusion: NEPHELE通过云端分布式计算与算法优化，显著提升了神经渲染的实时性与扩展性，为多用户协作场景提供了新解决方案。
search_text: NEPHELE: A Neural Platform for Highly Realistic Cloud Radiance Rendering NEPHELE：用于高度逼真的云辐射渲染的神经平台 We have recently seen tremendous progress in neural rendering (NR) advances, i.e., NeRF, for photo-real free-view synthesis. Yet, as a local technique based on a single computer/GPU, even the best-engineered Instant-NGP or i-NGP cannot reach real-time performance when rendering at a high resolution, and often requires huge local computing resources. In this paper, we resort to cloud rendering and present NEPHELE, a neural platform for highly realistic cloud radiance rendering. In stark contrast with existing NR approaches, our NEPHELE allows for more powerful rendering capabilities by combining multiple remote GPUs and facilitates collaboration by allowing multiple people to view the same NeRF scene simultaneously. We introduce i-NOLF to employ opacity light fields for ultra-fast neural radiance rendering in a one-query-per-ray manner. We further resemble the Lumigraph with geometry proxies for fast ray querying and subsequently employ a small MLP to model the local opacity lumishperes for high-quality rendering. We also adopt Perfect Spatial Hashing in i-NOLF to enhance cache coherence. As a result, our i-NOLF achieves an order of magnitude performance gain in terms of efficiency than i-NGP, especially for the multi-user multi-viewpoint setting under cloud rendering scenarios. We further tailor a task scheduler accompanied by our i-NOLF representation and demonstrate the advance of our methodological design through a comprehensive cloud platform, consisting of a series of cooperated modules, i.e., render farms, task assigner, frame composer, and detailed streaming strategies. Using such a cloud platform compatible with neural rendering, we further showcase the capabilities of our cloud radiance rendering through a series of applications, ranging from cloud VR/AR rendering. 我们最近看到了神经渲染 (NR) 方面的巨大进步，即用于照片真实自由视图合成的 NeRF。然而，作为一种基于单台计算机/GPU的本地技术，即使是设计最好的Instant-NGP或i-NGP在高分辨率渲染时也无法达到实时性能，并且往往需要巨大的本地计算资源。在本文中，我们采用云渲染并提出了 NEPHELE，一个用于高度逼真的云辐射渲染的神经平台。与现有的 NR 方法形成鲜明对比，我们的 NEPHELE 通过组合多个远程 GPU 实现更强大的渲染功能，并通过允许多人同时查看同一 NeRF 场景来促进协作。我们引入 i-NOLF，以每条光线一次查询的方式采用不透明光场进行超快速神经辐射渲染。我们进一步类似于具有几何代理的 Lumigraph，用于快速光线查询，随后采用小型 MLP 对局部不透明度 lumishperes 进行建模，以实现高质量渲染。我们还在 i-NOLF 中采用 Perfect Spatial Hashing 来增强缓存一致性。因此，我们的i-NOLF在效率方面比i-NGP实现了一个数量级的性能增益，特别是对于云渲染场景下的多用户多视点设置。我们进一步定制了一个带有 i-NOLF 表示的任务调度器，并通过一个综合的云平台展示了我们方法设计的先进性，该平台由一系列协作模块组成，即渲染农场、任务分配器、帧编辑器和详细的流策略。利用这样一个兼容神经渲染的云平台，我们通过云VR/AR渲染等一系列应用进一步展示了我们的云辐射渲染的能力。 本文提出NEPHELE，一个基于云端的神经渲染平台，通过结合多GPU资源和优化算法实现高质量实时云辐射度渲染。 Haimin Luo,Siyuan Zhang,Fuqiang Zhao,Haotian Jing,Penghao Wang,Zhenxiao Yu,Dongxue Yan,Junran Ding,Boyuan Zhang,Qiang Hu,Shu Yin,Lan Xu,JIngyi Yu cs.GR,cs.CV,cs.DC

authors_text: Minyoung Hwang,Jaeyeon Jeong,Minsoo Kim,Yoonseon Oh,Songhwai Oh
title: Meta-Explore: Exploratory Hierarchical Vision-and-Language Navigation Using Scene Object Spectrum Grounding
title_zh: Meta-Explore：使用场景对象频谱接地的探索性分层视觉和语言导航
abstract: The main challenge in vision-and-language navigation (VLN) is how to understand natural-language instructions in an unseen environment. The main limitation of conventional VLN algorithms is that if an action is mistaken, the agent fails to follow the instructions or explores unnecessary regions, leading the agent to an irrecoverable path. To tackle this problem, we propose Meta-Explore, a hierarchical navigation method deploying an exploitation policy to correct misled recent actions. We show that an exploitation policy, which moves the agent toward a well-chosen local goal among unvisited but observable states, outperforms a method which moves the agent to a previously visited state. We also highlight the demand for imagining regretful explorations with semantically meaningful clues. The key to our approach is understanding the object placements around the agent in spectral-domain. Specifically, we present a novel visual representation, called scene object spectrum (SOS), which performs category-wise 2D Fourier transform of detected objects. Combining exploitation policy and SOS features, the agent can correct its path by choosing a promising local goal. We evaluate our method in three VLN benchmarks: R2R, SOON, and REVERIE. Meta-Explore outperforms other baselines and shows significant generalization performance. In addition, local goal search using the proposed spectral-domain SOS features significantly improves the success rate by 17.1% and SPL by 20.6% for the SOON benchmark.
summary: The main challenge in vision-and-language navigation (VLN) is how to understand natural-language instructions in an unseen environment. The main limitation of conventional VLN algorithms is that if an action is mistaken, the agent fails to follow the instructions or explores unnecessary regions, leading the agent to an irrecoverable path. To tackle this problem, we propose Meta-Explore, a hierarchical navigation method deploying an exploitation policy to correct misled recent actions. We show that an exploitation policy, which moves the agent toward a well-chosen local goal among unvisited but observable states, outperforms a method which moves the agent to a previously visited state. We also highlight the demand for imagining regretful explorations with semantically meaningful clues. The key to our approach is understanding the object placements around the agent in spectral-domain. Specifically, we present a novel visual representation, called scene object spectrum (SOS), which performs category-wise 2D Fourier transform of detected objects. Combining exploitation policy and SOS features, the agent can correct its path by choosing a promising local goal. We evaluate our method in three VLN benchmarks: R2R, SOON, and REVERIE. Meta-Explore outperforms other baselines and shows significant generalization performance. In addition, local goal search using the proposed spectral-domain SOS features significantly improves the success rate by 17.1% and SPL by 20.6% for the SOON benchmark.
abstract_zh: 视觉和语言导航（VLN）的主要挑战是如何在看不见的环境中理解自然语言指令。传统 VLN 算法的主要局限性是，如果动作错误，智能体将无法遵循指令或探索不必要的区域，从而导致智能体走向不可恢复的路径。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 Meta-Explore，这是一种分层导航方法，部署利用策略来纠正最近被误导的操作。我们证明了一种利用策略，该策略使智能体在未访问过但可观察的状态中朝着精心选择的本地目标移动，其性能优于将智能体移动到先前访问过的状态的方法。我们还强调了用语义上有意义的线索来想象令人遗憾的探索的需求。我们方法的关键是理解谱域中代理周围的对象放置。具体来说，我们提出了一种新颖的视觉表示，称为场景对象谱（SOS），它对检测到的对象执行按类别的 2D 傅里叶变换。结合利用策略和 SOS 功能，代理可以通过选择有希望的本地目标来纠正其路径。我们在三个 VLN 基准测试中评估我们的方法：R2R、SOON 和 REVERIE。 Meta-Explore 优于其他基线，并显示出显着的泛化性能。此外，使用所提出的谱域 SOS 特征的局部目标搜索将 SOON 基准的成功率显着提高了 17.1%，SPL 提高了 20.6%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Meta-Explore方法，通过场景物体光谱表示和探索策略解决视觉-语言导航中未见过环境下的路径纠错问题。
AI.motivation: 传统VLN算法在路径错误时难以恢复，且探索效率低，需通过语义线索进行有效路径修正。
AI.method: 设计分层导航框架，结合基于光谱域的场景物体光谱（SOS）表示与探索策略，通过傅里叶变换提取物体分布特征并选择局部目标进行路径修正。
AI.result: 在R2R、SOON和REVERIE基准测试中超越其他方法，SOON基准成功率提升17.1%，SPL提升20.6%。
AI.conclusion: Meta-Explore通过SOS特征与探索策略的结合，显著提升了VLN任务的路径规划鲁棒性和泛化能力。

authors_text: Matthew Cavorsi,Orhan Eren Akgün,Michal Yemini,Andrea Goldsmith,Stephanie Gil
title: Exploiting Trust for Resilient Hypothesis Testing with Malicious Robots (evolved version)
title_zh: 利用恶意机器人的弹性假设检验信任（进化版）
comment: 21 pages, 5 figures, 1 table. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2209.12285
comments: 21 pages, 5 figures, 1 table. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2209.12285
abstract: We develop a resilient binary hypothesis testing framework for decision making in adversarial multi-robot crowdsensing tasks. This framework exploits stochastic trust observations between robots to arrive at tractable, resilient decision making at a centralized Fusion Center (FC) even when i) there exist malicious robots in the network and their number may be larger than the number of legitimate robots, and ii) the FC uses one-shot noisy measurements from all robots. We derive two algorithms to achieve this. The first is the Two Stage Approach (2SA) that estimates the legitimacy of robots based on received trust observations, and provably minimizes the probability of detection error in the worst-case malicious attack. Here, the proportion of malicious robots is known but arbitrary. For the case of an unknown proportion of malicious robots, we develop the Adversarial Generalized Likelihood Ratio Test (A-GLRT) that uses both the reported robot measurements and trust observations to estimate the trustworthiness of robots, their reporting strategy, and the correct hypothesis simultaneously. We exploit special problem structure to show that this approach remains computationally tractable despite several unknown problem parameters. We deploy both algorithms in a hardware experiment where a group of robots conducts crowdsensing of traffic conditions on a mock-up road network similar in spirit to Google Maps, subject to a Sybil attack. We extract the trust observations for each robot from actual communication signals which provide statistical information on the uniqueness of the sender. We show that even when the malicious robots are in the majority, the FC can reduce the probability of detection error to 30.5% and 29% for the 2SA and the A-GLRT respectively.
summary: We develop a resilient binary hypothesis testing framework for decision making in adversarial multi-robot crowdsensing tasks. This framework exploits stochastic trust observations between robots to arrive at tractable, resilient decision making at a centralized Fusion Center (FC) even when i) there exist malicious robots in the network and their number may be larger than the number of legitimate robots, and ii) the FC uses one-shot noisy measurements from all robots. We derive two algorithms to achieve this. The first is the Two Stage Approach (2SA) that estimates the legitimacy of robots based on received trust observations, and provably minimizes the probability of detection error in the worst-case malicious attack. Here, the proportion of malicious robots is known but arbitrary. For the case of an unknown proportion of malicious robots, we develop the Adversarial Generalized Likelihood Ratio Test (A-GLRT) that uses both the reported robot measurements and trust observations to estimate the trustworthiness of robots, their reporting strategy, and the correct hypothesis simultaneously. We exploit special problem structure to show that this approach remains computationally tractable despite several unknown problem parameters. We deploy both algorithms in a hardware experiment where a group of robots conducts crowdsensing of traffic conditions on a mock-up road network similar in spirit to Google Maps, subject to a Sybil attack. We extract the trust observations for each robot from actual communication signals which provide statistical information on the uniqueness of the sender. We show that even when the malicious robots are in the majority, the FC can reduce the probability of detection error to 30.5% and 29% for the 2SA and the A-GLRT respectively.
abstract_zh: 我们开发了一个弹性二元假设检验框架，用于对抗性多机器人众感知任务中的决策。该框架利用机器人之间的随机信任观察，在集中式融合中心 (FC) 中做出易于处理、有弹性的决策，即使 i) 网络中存在恶意机器人且其数量可能大于合法机器人的数量，以及 ii) FC 使用来自所有机器人的一次性噪声测量。我们推导出两种算法来实现这一目标。第一个是两阶段方法（2SA），它根据收到的信任观察来估计机器人的合法性，并可证明最大限度地减少最坏情况恶意攻击中检测错误的概率。这里，恶意机器人的比例是已知的，但是是任意的。对于恶意机器人比例未知的情况，我们开发了对抗性广义似然比测试（A-GLRT），它使用报告的机器人测量值和信任观察来同时估计机器人的可信度、其报告策略和正确的假设。我们利用特殊的问题结构来表明，尽管存在一些未知的问题参数，但这种方法在计算上仍然易于处理。我们在硬件实验中部署了这两种算法，其中一组机器人在类似于 Google 地图的模拟道路网络上对交通状况进行群体感知，并受到 Sybil 攻击。我们从实际通信信号中提取每个机器人的信任观察结果，这些信号提供有关发送者唯一性的统计信息。我们表明，即使恶意机器人占多数，FC 也可以将 2SA 和 A-GLRT 的检测错误概率分别降低至 30.5% 和 29%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种在对抗性多机器人众包传感任务中利用机器人之间信任关系的鲁棒二元假设检验框架，以应对恶意机器人攻击。
AI.motivation: 在多机器人协作任务中，恶意机器人可能数量超过合法机器人，且融合中心仅能获取单次噪声测量值，导致决策易受攻击。
AI.method: 开发了两种算法：Two Stage Approach（2SA）通过信任观测估计机器人合法性，以及Adversarial Generalized Likelihood Ratio Test（A-GLRT）联合估计机器人可信度、报告策略和假设。
AI.result: 在硬件实验中，当恶意机器人占多数时，2SA和A-GLRT将检测错误概率分别降低至30.5%和29%。
AI.conclusion: 利用信任观测的框架能有效降低对抗环境下的检测错误率，实现鲁棒决策，即使恶意机器人占优时仍保持较高可靠性。
